| Show Stereotypes | Text and Icon | Text and IconText IconShape Image and TextShape ImageDo Not Display | Display stereotypes on symbols. |
| Show DSL Stereotypes | All | AllOnly LastNone | Specify whether to show all, none, or only the last of the DSL Stereotypes (stereotypes that have DSL customization with the option hideMetatype = true) on a symbol. |
| Show Constraints | true | true/false | Displays constraints on symbols. |
| Show Owner | Do Not Display | Do Not DisplayBelow Element NameIn Same Line With NameAbove Element Name | Displays the name of the owner model element (beneath the corresponding model element name - old style notation, in the same line with the name, or above the element name). |
| Wrap Words | false | true/false | Wraps words to a new line when text exceeds textbox (name, constraint, tagged value, conveyed information, extension point, or provided and required interfaces) width. |
| Show Tagged Values | true | true/false | Displays tagged values on symbols. |
| Tagged Values and Constraints Horizontal Position | Right | CenterLeftRight | Choose the text alignment position of tagged values and constraints on the shape. |
| Constraint Text Mode | Expression | NameExpressionName and Expression | Choose the constraint name or expression to be displayed on the symbol. |
| Show Element Properties | false | true/false | Choose if the properties are displayed on the symbol of the element. |
| Show Icons in Compartments | true | true/false | Set to true to show the icon before the element name in the compartment. |
| Show Element Type in Compartments | false | true/false | Set to true to show the element type before its name when the icons are hidden in the compartment. |
| Show Derived Sign | false | true/false | Set to true to show the derived sign (/) before the element name in the Tagged Values compartment. |
| Paths |  |  |  |
| Fill Color | RGB [255, 255, 204] | Custom | Change the fill color of the shape. |
| Use Fill Color | true | true/false | Shapes are colored on the diagram. |
| Pen Color | RGB [66, 66, 66] | Custom | Change the color of the path or lines around shapes. |
| Text Color | RGB [0, 0, 0] | Custom | Change the color of the text. |
| Line Width | 1 | Real | Specify the line width of the symbol. |
| Font | Arial 11 | multi-list | Change the font style of the text. |
| Show Number Tag Name | true | true/false | The option allows you to turn on or turn off the element number tag name on the symbol. |
| Element Number Display Mode | Before the element name | Above the element nameBefore the element nameDo not display on symbol | The element number display mode option specifies the position on the symbol where the element number should be displayed. |
| Path Style | Rectilinear | RectilinearOblique Bezier | Choose the style for the selected path. |
| Rounded Corners | false | true/false | Path breakpoints rounded or not. |
| Constraint Text Mode | Expression | NameExpressionName and Expression | Choose the constraint name or expression to be displayed on the symbol. |
| Suspend Auto-Resizing of Shape | Same as Diagram | Same as Diagramtruefalse | Set to true to keep the original size of the selected shape while editing the model by cutting text to fit the shape. You can use the Resize to Fit Content command (a small black square on the bottom line of the selected shape) to adjust the size of the shape to fit the content completely. By default, the appropriate property defined for the diagram is used. |
| Suspend Auto-Displaying of Label | Same as Diagram | Same as Diagramtruefalse | Set to true if you do not want to automatically show labels on the diagram pane while editing the model. You can use the Show Suspended labels command (a small black square on the selected path) to show suspended labels on the diagram pane. By default, the appropriate property defined for the diagram is used. |
| Label Orientation | Horizontal | HorizontalVerticalAutomatic | Specify the orientation of labels. Automatic label orientation will rotate labels of symbols according to the symbol orientation or edge orientation on which the symbol is placed. |
| Diagram |  |  |  |
| Pen Color | RGB [66, 66, 66] | Custom | Change the color of the path or lines around shapes. |
| Text Color | RGB [0, 0, 0] | Custom | Change the color of the text. |
| Line Width | 1 | Real | Specify the line width of the symbol. |
| Font | Arial 11 | multi-list | Change the font style of the text. |
| Show Number Tag Name | true | true/false | The option allows you to turn on or turn off the element number tag name on the symbol. |
| Element Number Display Mode | Before the element name | Above the element nameBefore the element nameDo not display on symbol | The element number display mode option specifies the position on the symbol where the element number should be displayed. |
| Background Color | RGB [255, 255, 255] | Custom | Change the color of the diagram background. |
| Use Gradient Fill | true | true/false | Use gradient for the shape color. |
| 3D Shadow | true | true/false | Display 3D shadow on shapes. |
| Show Grid | false | true/false | Displays gridlines on the diagram background. |
| Grid Size | 7 | 2-30 | Define the desired size for the gridlines. |
| Snap Paths to Grid | true | true/false | When moving paths, snap them to the grid. |
| Snap Shapes to Grid | true | true/false | When moving shapes, snap them to the grid. |
| Show Message Numbers | true | true/false | Show message numbers on the diagram. |
| Use Advanced Numbering | true | true/false | Use advanced numbering. |
| Show Activations | true | true/false | Display activation bars on lifelines in sequence diagrams. |
| Show Diagram Info | false | true/false | Display the table with the main information (name, author, creation and modification dates, etc.) about the diagram. |
| Show Owner | false | true/false | Displays diagram owner on the diagram tab. |
| Show Stereotypes | Text and Icon | Text and IconText IconShape Image and TextShape ImageDo Not Display | Display stereotypes on symbols. |
| Show DSL Stereotypes | All | AllOnly LastNone | Specify whether to show all, none, or only the last of the DSL Stereotypes (stereotypes that have DSL customization with the option hideMetatype = true) on a symbol. |
| Use Stereotype | Diagram | ContextDiagram | Use diagram stereotype or diagram context stereotype. |
| Stereotype Color | RGB [0, 0, 0] | Custom | Change the color of the stereotype text label. |
| Stereotype Font | Arial 11 | multi-list | Change the font style of the stereotype text label. |
| Diagram Orientation | Vertical | VerticalHorizontal | Set a diagram orientation mode. |
| Add Line Jumps To | Horizontal Line | NoneHorizontal LineVertial Line | Specified how line jumps are added to intersections of links on the diagram. None - specifies that the diagram use no line jumps. Horizontal Line - specified that the line jumps appear only on horizontal lines. Vertical Line - specified that the line jumps appear only on the vertical lines. |
| Suspend Auto-Resizing of Shape | Same as Diagram | Same as Diagramtruefalse | Set to true to keep the original size of the selected shape while editing the model by cutting text to fit the shape. You can use the Resize to Fit Content command (a small black square on the bottom line of the selected shape) to adjust the size of the shape to fit the content completely. By default, the appropriate property defined for the diagram is used. |
| Suspend Auto-Displaying of Label | Same as Diagram | Same as Diagramtruefalse | Set to true if you do not want to automatically show labels on the diagram pane while editing the model. You can use the Show Suspended labels command (a small black square on the selected path) to show suspended labels on the diagram pane. By default, the appropriate property defined for the diagram is used. |
| Show Diagram Frame | true | true/false | Displays diagram frame on the diagram. |
| Show Abbreviated Type | false | true/false | Shows full/abbreviated diagram keyword type on the diagram frame header. |
| Show Diagram Name | true | true/false | The diagram name is displayed or hidden in the diagram frame header. |
| Show Diagram Type | false | true/false | The diagram kind is displayed or hidden in the diagram frame header. |
| Show Parameters | true | true/false | The diagram context element parameters (if there are any) are displayed in the diagram frame header. |
| Show Context Name | true | true/false | The diagram context element name is displayed or hidden in the diagram frame header. |
| Show Context Type | false | true/false | The diagram context element type is displayed or hidden in the diagram frame header. |
| Show Context Kind | true | true/false | The context kind is a keyword predefined in UML (e.g., package, class, activity). |
| Autosize | false | true/false | Adjusts the size of a symbol to the contained information so that it uses minimum space. |
| Aspect Ratio | Unspecified | Unspecified1:14:33:416:99:16A4 LandscapeA4 PortraitUS Letter LandscapeUS Letter Portrait | Choose the diagram frame aspect ratio from the list or define a new one. the format of your defined aspect ratio should be 'W:H'. |
| Use Rounded Corners | true | true/false | Use rounded corners on the diagram frame. Diagram frames in Activity and State Machine diagrams can be represented as rounded rectangles. |
| Suspend Auto-Resizing of Shapes | Same as Diagram | Same as Diagramtruefalse | Set to true to keep the original size of shapes while editing the model by cutting text to fit inside the shape. You can use the Resize to Fit Content command (a small black square on the bottom line of the selected shape) to adjust the size of the shape to fit the content completely. |
| Stereotypes |  |  |  |
| Create elements' properties by stereotype. These properties will be applied when the symbol of the stereotyped element is created on the diagram pane. |  |  |  |
| Default model properties |  |  |  |
| In the Default model properties tab, select an element and change its default property values (the element will have these values after its creation). To change the default value only for a specific diagram, go to Diagrams main menu / Customize and edit the selected element properties. |  |  |  |
| Code Engineering |  |  |  |
| Set general code generation or code reversing options. Also, configure generated code formatting styles for the selected programming languages. Subcategories:Code GenerationReverseJava Language OptionsC++ Language OptionsCORBA IDL 3.0 Language Options |  |  |  |

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>Use the <strong>Project Options</strong> dialog to do the following:</p><ul><li>Specify general project-specific options.</li><li>Specify summarizing information (for example, diagram author, diagram creation, and modification dates) that will be displayed on each diagram.</li><li><p>Specify symbol property styles for shapes, paths, diagrams, and stereotypes within the project.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="71882ce9-ca8f-410d-8418-76da523dc1f5"><ac:rich-text-body><p>For instructions on how to create, edit, clone, import /export, or remove symbol property styles, please refer to <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Style Engine</span>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li></ul><ul><li><p>Change default element property values.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="49c328ba-8acb-4017-b343-59d8c9ad89f6"><ac:rich-text-body><p>For the instructions about setting the default element property values, please refer to <span>Default Property Values.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li></ul><ul><li>Set general code generation or code reversing options as well as code formatting styles for selected programming languages.</li></ul><p><br /></p><p>The <strong>Project Options</strong> dialog includes option groups, each designated for one of the above-mentioned features and containing lists of corresponding options. Groups are represented using the tree structure.</p><p>An option value can be changed by typing a new value, setting a value to true/false, or selecting a value from the list. Click the <strong>Reset to Defaults</strong> button to restore default option values.</p><p><br class="atl-forced-newline" />To open the <strong>Project Options</strong> dialog</p><hr /><ul><li>From the <strong>Options</strong> menu, select <strong>Project</strong>.<br /><br /><ac:image ac:title="Structure of Project Options dialog" ac:alt="Structure of Project Options dialog"><ri:attachment ri:filename="structure_of_project_options_dialog.png" /></ac:image></li></ul><p><br /></p><h3>Changing options</h3><p>To change a desired project option, you need to find it in the <strong>Project Options</strong> dialog. These options are grouped into categories to make it easier for you to locate them. Use the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Quick filter" ri:space-key="MDTWRT" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[q]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Quick filter" ri:space-key="MDTWRT" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[uick filter]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> to find the desired option in the list. Filtering is performed in all available options, not only in the selected option group.</p><p><br class="atl-forced-newline" />To make sure you selected the right option, first read its description and see if the effect of changing the option value is what you expect to be done.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f0e54e23-1723-4c29-ab7b-b6ee27c57d9a"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Ensure the Show Description mode is turned on in the <strong>Project Options</strong> dialog. <br />To turn the Show Description mode on or off, click the Show Description button on the following toolbar in the <strong>Project Options</strong> dialog.</p><p><ac:image ac:title="The Show Description button in the Project Options toolbar" ac:alt="The Show Description button in the Project Options toolbar"><ri:attachment ri:filename="options_group_toolbar.png" /></ac:image></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br class="atl-forced-newline" />To read the option description</p><hr /><ol><li>Click an option that you want to change.</li><li>Read the option description in the area below the options list.</li></ol><h3 class="auto-cursor-target">Project Options</h3><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="894362a7-7ba8-4d42-9b4e-d7a987937a9d"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The table below displays the default project options. Additional options may appear based on installed plugins.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th style="width: 15.8529%;">Option</th><th style="width: 17.8038%;">Default value</th><th style="width: 10.3634%;">Value options</th><th style="width: 55.98%;">Description</th></tr><tr><td class="highlight-#deebff" colspan="4" data-highlight-colour="#deebff" style="width: 100.0%;" title="Background color : Light blue 35%"><strong>General</strong></td></tr><tr><td class="highlight-#f4f5f7" colspan="4" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7" style="width: 100.0%;" title="Background color : Light grey 100%">Browser</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Group Composite Instances</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">true</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">true/false</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Set to true to show composite instances under their owning instances instead of the actual owning package in the Containment tree.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Show Diagram Abbreviations</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">false</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">true/false</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Set to true to display abbreviations in front of the diagram names in the Containment Tree. <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12PTWRT" ri:content-title="Show diagram abbreviation in Containment tree" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more >>>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></td></tr><tr><td class="highlight-#deebff" colspan="4" data-highlight-colour="#deebff" title="Background colour : Light blue 35%">Concept Modeling</td></tr><tr><td class="highlight-#f4f5f7" colspan="4" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7" title="Background colour : Light grey 100%">Concept Modeling</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Preferred annotation property</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">-</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">custom</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">The preferred annotation property for comment providing documentation. When nothing is selected, '<a>http://purl.org/dc/terms/description'</a> will be used.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Always ask to remove generalization set names</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">true</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">true/false</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Opening a project with named generalization sets will prompt to automatically remove names of generalization sets.</td></tr><tr><td class="highlight-#f4f5f7" colspan="4" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7" title="Background colour : Light grey 100%">OWL Import/Export</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Always ask for a file destination when exporting to OWL</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">true</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">true/false</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Turns off use of the default file path and opens a file explorer window of OWL files.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Generate label when  exporting to OWL</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">Ask</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;"><p>Always generate a matching label</p><p>Never generate a matching label </p><p>Ask</p></td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Preferred option for generating labels when exporting to OWL. &quot;Always generate a matching label&quot; will generate a label when it would match the end of the element's IRI. &quot;Never generate a matching label&quot; will not generate a label when it would match the end of an element's IRI. &quot;Ask&quot; will prompt for every export.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Freeze IRI when exporting OWL</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">Ask</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;"><p>Always freeze before export</p><p>Never freeze before export</p><p>Ask</p></td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Preferred option for freezing IRIs when exporting OWL. &quot;Always freeze before export&quot; will silently set missing IRI values. &quot;Never freeze before export&quot; will not set missing IRI values. &quot;Ask&quot; will prompt for every exporting package that has elements with missing IRI values.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">OWL Export Syntax</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">RDF/XML</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;"><p>RDF/XML</p><p>JSON-LD</p><p>OWL Functional</p><p>Turtle</p><p>Manchester</p></td><td style="width: 55.98%;">The preferred syntax for OWL export.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">OWL Import Catalog</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">-</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">custom</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">An Oasis-*standard catalog file to map IRIs to local files or other IRIs. See <a href="https://www.oasis-open.org/committees/download.php/14809/xml-catalogs.html">https://www.oasis-open.org/committees/download.php/14809/xml-catalogs.html</a> for more information.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">URI Construction Strategy</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">Hash URI</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;"><p>Hash URI</p><p>303 URI</p></td><td style="width: 55.98%;">The preferred URI construction strategy, as defined in HTTP://<a href="http://www.w3org/TR/cooluris/">www.w3org/TR/cooluris/</a>.</td></tr><tr><td class="highlight-#f4f5f7" colspan="4" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7" title="Background colour : Light grey 100%">Glossary Table</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Add classes to the glossary</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">false</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">true/false</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Generate a glossary entry for classes.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Add association ends to the glossary</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">false</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">true/false</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Generate a glossary entry for association ends.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Add attributes to the glossary</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">false</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">true/false</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Generate a glossary entry for attributes.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Add enumerations to the glossary</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">false</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">true/false</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Generate a glossary entry for enumerations.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Add enumeration literals to the glossary</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">false</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">true/false</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Generate a glossary entry for enumeration literals.</td></tr><tr><td class="highlight-#f4f5f7" colspan="4" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7" title="Background colour : Light grey 100%">Natural Language Glossary Report</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Natural Language Glossary annotation property list</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">-</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">custom</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">An ordered list of annotation properties to include in every Natural Language Glossary entry, in addition to preferred annotation property used for the definition.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Include property definitions in the Natural Language Glossary</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">true</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">true/false</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Lists property definitions in addition to class definitions in the Natural Language Glossary.</td></tr><tr><td class="highlight-#f4f5f7" colspan="4" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7" style="width: 100.0%;" title="Background color : Light grey 100%">Corba IDL</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">CORBA Interfaces implemented as</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">UML Interface</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;"><p>UML Class</p><p>UML Interface</p></td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Specify whether the CORBA Interfaces should be implemented as a UML Class or UML Interface.</td></tr><tr><td class="highlight-#f4f5f7" colspan="4" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7" title="Background colour : Light grey 100%">DDL</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Show relationship ends as</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">No special notation</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;"><p>No special notation</p><p>With multiplicities</p><p>Crow's foot</p></td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Possibility to change relationship's display mode in IMM diagrams.</td></tr><tr><td class="highlight-#f4f5f7" colspan="4" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7" style="width: 100.0%;" title="Background color : Light grey 100%">Dependency Checker</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Check for Cyclic Dependencies among Used Projects</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">false</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">true/false</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Set to true to check for cyclic dependencies during the project export or package share. </td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Dependency Checker Severity Level</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">Info</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;"><p>Info</p><p>Warning</p><p>Error</p></td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Specify the dependency checker severity. The dependencies of the specified and higher severity will be displayed.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Ignore Standard/System Profiles</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">true</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">true/false</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Set to true to exclude standard/system profiles when checking for cyclic dependencies in used projects.</td></tr><tr><td class="highlight-#f4f5f7" colspan="4" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7" style="width: 100.0%;" title="Background color : Light grey 100%">Diagrams</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Use i18n property for text rendering in the diagrams</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">true</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">true/false</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Set to false to disable the i18n property. If the i18n property is set to false, all text symbols that are displayed in the diagram pane (for example, text which is displayed on the symbol) will not be distorted at any zoom ratio. </td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Display All Pins in Diagrams</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">true</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">true/false</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Set to true to always display pin elements in the diagrams. Note: if this option is set to true and the pin symbol is deleted from the diagram, then the corresponding pin element will be deleted from the model, too.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Display All Activity Parameter Nodes in Diagrams</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">true</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">true/false</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Set to true to always display activity parameter node elements in the diagrams. Note: if this option is set to true and the activity parameter node symbol is deleted from the diagram, then the corresponding activity parameter node element will be deleted from the model, too. </td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Use different Join/Fork and Decision/Merge notations</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">true</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">true/false</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Use different notations to draw Join/Fork and Decision/Merge.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Hide Information Flow If Conveyed Information is Missing</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">false</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">true/false</td><td style="width: 55.98%;"><p>Set to true to hide the arrow notation that adorns the realizing element if the Conveyed Information of a realized Information Flow is hidden or unspecified.</p><p><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12PTWRT" ri:content-title="Working with exchanges" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more >>>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p><p><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="SYSMLPTWRT" ri:content-title="Setting project options" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more >>>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p></td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Diagram Context</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">Create Automatically</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;"><p>Create Automatically</p><p>Do Not Create</p></td><td style="width: 55.98%;">A new diagram context element will be created automatically when the new diagram owned by a Package is created. This option is valid for all diagrams based on a Composite Structure Diagram.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Apply Image To</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">Type and Notify</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;"><p>Type and Notify</p><p>Type</p><p>Property</p></td><td style="width: 55.98%;">(This is personal property, meaning its value is unique for each user). Specify whether to apply an image to the property or its type when dragging or selecting an image from the Image Library or other resource. This option is available when the Type Selection Mode is switched on.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Use Aspects</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">false</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">true/false</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Set to true to use definable diagram aspects in the project.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Ignored Aspects</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">-</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">multi-selection</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Specify the aspects which should not be used in the project.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Suspend Auto-Resizing of Shapes</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">true</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">true/false</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Set to true to keep the original size of shapes while editing the model by cutting text to fit inside the shape. You can use the Resize to Fit Content command (a small black square on the bottom line of the selected shape) to adjust the size of the shape to fit the content completely.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Suspend Auto-Displaying of Labels</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">true</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">true/false</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Set to true if you do not want to automatically show labels on the diagram pane while editing the model. You can use the Show Suspended Labels command (a small black square on the selected path) to show suspended labels on the diagram pane.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Highlight Suspended Symbols</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">true</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">true/false</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Set to true to highlight suspended symbols with not completely visible content.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Time Limit to Build Diagram </td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">60</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">Real</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Specify the maximum time (in seconds) allotted for diagram building while exporting a diagram. If the time limit is exceeded, the diagram is not exported. Set to 0 to export the diagram without the time limit. </td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Image Height Limit</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">10000</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">Real</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Specify the maximum image height (in pixels) for diagram export. If the height limit is exceeded, the exported diagram is cropped, or the diagram is not exported at all, depending on the selected preference. Set to 0 to export the diagram without the image height limit. </td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Image Width Limit</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">10000</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">Real</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Specify the maximum image width (in pixels) for diagram export. If the width limit is exceeded, the exported diagram is cropped, or the diagram is not exported at all, depending on the selected preference. Set to 0 to export the diagram without the image width limit. </td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Export Preference When Limits Exceeded</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">Export Cropped Diagram</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;"><p>Export Cropped Diagram</p><p>Do Not Export</p></td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Specify the diagram export preference if the diagram image height and/or width limits are exceeded.</td></tr><tr><td class="highlight-#f4f5f7" colspan="4" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7" style="width: 100.0%;" title="Background color : Light grey 100%">Element References</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Default Element Hyperlink Text Display Mode</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">Name</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;"><p>Name</p><p>Icon + Representation text</p><p>Representation text</p><p>Custom text</p></td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Specify how the hyperlink to the model element is displayed by default. Select 'Name' to display the element name from its Specification window. The 'Representation text' displays the full element title, visible in the Containment tree. Choose the 'Icon+Representation text' to display the element icon together with the full element title. Select 'Custom text' to type your own text.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Default Element Hyperlink Text Update Mode</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">Automatically updated</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;"><p>Automatically updated</p><p>Automatically checked</p><p>Manually checked</p><p>Do not update</p></td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Specify when the hyperlink to the model element will be updated by default if the referenced element and the hyperlink become inconsistent. Select 'Automatically updated' to update hyperlink text or the referenced element name automatically. Choose 'Automatically checked' to enable active validation rules which check if the hyperlink and the referenced element are coherent. The 'Manually checked' option allows you to run validation manually, while the 'Do not update' option means that validation will not find any inconsistencies.</td></tr><tr><td class="highlight-#f4f5f7" colspan="4" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7" style="width: 100.0%;" title="Background color : Light grey 100%">General</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Path to Used Project</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;"><p>&lt;project.dir&gt;</p><p>&lt;install.root&gt;\profiles</p><p>&lt;install.root&gt;\modelLibraries</p></td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">Custom</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Specify the paths to store used projects and extension elements.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Enable Dot Notation for Associations</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">false</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">true/false</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Set to true to use the dot notation for associations. If the dot notation is applied to an association, the association end is decorated with the dot if that end is owned by a classifier. The absence of the dot signifies ownership by the association.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Change Ownership of Non-Navigable Association End when Changing Navigability</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">true</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">true/false</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Set to true to automatically change ownership of the non-navigable association end when the navigability of the association was changed.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Qualified Name Display Style</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">Model Library Relative</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;"><p>Absolute</p><p>Model Relative</p><p>Model Library Relative</p><p>Model or Model Library Relative</p></td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Specify the mode of displaying the element qualified name. If the Model Library Relative mode is specified, it means that the element's full qualified name hierarchy is displayed on the shape, starting from the </td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Synchronize Parameters and Arguments</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">true</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">true/false</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Set to true to synchronize the associated elements on elements creation or update.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Layout Template Creation Mode</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">Definition</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;"><p>Usage</p><p>Definition</p></td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Specify the mode layout template diagram creation. Choose 'Definition' to create the layout templates as a class diagram. Choose 'Usage' to create the layout templates as a composite structure diagram.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Layout Use Case Scenario Activity Diagram</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">false</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">true/false</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Set to true to automatically lay out the activity diagram of the use case scenario every time it is opened.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Decimal Places</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">4</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">Real</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Specify the maximum number of decimal places to be used when displaying the real number in tables and element Specification windows.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Eclipse UML2 XMI Output Location</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">-</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">Custom</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Specify the path to the location where the exported Eclipse UML2 XMI will be saved.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Use Glossary</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">true</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">true/false</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Set to true to use the glossary functionality: mark terms in the text, create terms directly from the text, or reuse them in newly created texts.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Show Excel/CSV Import Migration Message</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">true</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">true/false</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">If true, the Migration message is shown each time when importing Excel/CSV mapping created with the CSV Import plugin.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Tooltips Style</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">Do Not Show</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;"><p>Do Not Show</p><p>Show Element Name</p><p>Show Element Documentation</p></td><td style="width: 55.98%;">(This is personal property, meaning its value is unique for each user). Specify the mode to display the element tooltip.</td></tr><tr><td class="highlight-#f4f5f7" colspan="4" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7" style="width: 100.0%;" title="Background color : Light grey 100%">Legends</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Filtering Preference</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">Show</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;"><p>Show</p><p>Hide</p></td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Specify the default filtering preference for Legends.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Use Legends</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">true</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">true/false</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Set to true to enable Legends adorning and filtering functionalities across all tables and diagrams with symbols in the project.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Ignored Legends</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">-</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">multi-selection</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Specify the Legends which should not be used in the project.</td></tr><tr><td class="highlight-#f4f5f7" colspan="4" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7" style="width: 100.0%;" title="Background color : Light grey 100%">Numbering</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Use Element Auto-Number</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">false</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">true/false</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Element auto-numbering option to control (turn off or turn on) element numbering functionality for the whole project.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Display Element Number</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">false</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">true/false</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">The 'Display Element Number' option allows to turn on or turn off the representation of the element number.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Lock Element Auto-Numbering</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">false</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">true/false</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">If set to true, the element auto-numbering is locked and cannot be changed.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Check Element Number Uniqueness Including all Properties</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">false</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">true/false</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Set to true to include the main element number together with all internal element numbers into element uniqueness checking.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Check Element Number Uniqueness</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">true</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">true/false</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Set to true to check element number uniqueness.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Check Element Number Uniqueness in:</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">-</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">multi-selection</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Element number uniqueness options allow specifying where your element number should be unique. </td></tr><tr><td class="highlight-#f4f5f7" colspan="4" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7" title="Background colour : Light grey 100%">ReqIF</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Path to the resources</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">&lt;project.dir&gt;</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">custom</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Specify the path to ReqIF resources to allow for exchanging projects among users. The default location is the project directory &lt;project.dir&gt;. Absolut local path, URL, and path variables are supported.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Store ReqIF Metadata Information</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">false</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">true/false</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Set to true to store the ReqIF metadata information in the model.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Relationship Types</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">-</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">custom</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Specify the relationship types to export. Otherwise, all relationship types are exported.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Pre-processing Rules </td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">-</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">custom</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Select rules to apply before export.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Exclude Properties</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">A list of properties</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">-</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Add properties to ignore and not export into the ReqIF file.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Element Type</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">-</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">custom</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Specify an element type to create for the objects you are importing. The default type, unless otherwise specified, is Requirement.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Relation Type</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">-</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">custom</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Specify a relationship type to create for the relationships you are importing. The default type, unless otherwise specified, is DerivedReqt.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Post-processing Rules</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">-</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">custom</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Select rules to apply after import.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Import All Properties</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">true</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">true/false</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Set to true to import all the properties into the model.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Excluded Properties</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">A list of properties</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">-</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Add properties to ignore and not import into the project.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Properties to Ignore for Status Update</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">-</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">custom</td><td style="width: 55.98%;"><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">Specify the properties that, when modified, will not change the elements' status to 'updated'.</span></td></tr><tr><td class="highlight-#f4f5f7" colspan="4" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7" title="Background colour : Light grey 100%">Requirements</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Unredline Patterns in Requirement Text</td><td>false</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">true/false</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Set to true to underline the Requirement text that matches the predefined pattern.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Show Reuse Dialog when Deriving Requirement</td><td>true</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">true/false</td><td style="width: 55.98%;"><p>(This is personal property, meaning its value is unique for each user)</p><p>Show a dialog before reusing the name and text when creating a new Derive relation.</p></td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Reuse Requirement Name and Text</td><td>true</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">true/false</td><td style="width: 55.98%;"><p>(This is personal property, meaning its value is unique for each user)</p><p>Reuse name and text when creating a new Derive relation.</p></td></tr><tr><td class="highlight-#f4f5f7" colspan="4" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7" title="Background colour : Light grey 100%"><p title="">Styling</p></td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Ask to update outdated 'Default' symbol styles</td><td>true</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">true/false</td><td style="width: 55.98%;"><p>Set to true to ask to update outdated 'Default' symbol styles.</p></td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Ask to update outdated 'Defined Elsewhere' symbol styles</td><td>true</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">true/false</td><td style="width: 55.98%;"><p>Set to true to ask to update outdated 'Defined Elsewhere' symbol styles.</p></td></tr><tr><td class="highlight-#f4f5f7" colspan="4" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7" style="width: 100.0%;" title="Background color : Light grey 100%">Suspect Links</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Enable Tracking</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">false</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">true/false</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Set to true to start tracking suspect links.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Track Deprecated Elements</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">true</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">true/false</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Set to true to track element deletions.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Track Links in main Project Only</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">true</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">true/false</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Set to true to track suspect links in the main project only.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Tracking Scope</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">-</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">multi-selection</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Select elements whose suspicions should be tracked.</td></tr><tr><td class="highlight-#f4f5f7" colspan="4" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7" title="Background colour : Light grey 100%">SysML</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Propagate SysML Values</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">true</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">true/false</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">If True, the propagation mechanism is turned on which allows value reconfiguration from the nested structures of the context. Propagated values will be displayed in the initialValue compartment. Set to False to turn off the Value Propagation mechanism. Then the values will be collected only from the specified instances.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Change allocated Behavior context</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">true</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">true/false</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">If enabled, Behavior::context will be the Block to which behavior is allocated.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Rename Property on Type Change</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">false</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">true/false</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">If set to true, the name of the property or port (except Association End) that is default tool-generated is automatically changed to a type name after specifying the type for them. </td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Rename Association End on Type Change</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">true</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">true/false</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">If set to true, the name of the Association End that is default tool-generated is automatically named as a type specified at the role end after connecting or reconnecting the Association. </td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Create Association on Part Creation</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">false</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">true/false</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">If enabled, an association between a part property type and part owner is created automatically.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Include Directed Features into Combined Direction of Proxy Port</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">false</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">true/false</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">If enabled, includes all owned and inherited Directed Features of the property type into derived combined direction of the Proxy Port. Tread Provided as Out, Required as In direction. </td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Show Units</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">false</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">true/false</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">If enabled, the units are shown next to values and can be specified or changed directly on a Value Specification.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Show Type Names in Property Path</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">false</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">true/false</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Use type name in property path &quot;dot notation&quot; when property name is empty.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Allocation Mode</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">Ask and Remember </td><td style="width: 10.3634%;"><p>Definition (Activity allocated to Block)</p><p>Usage (Activity allocated to Part)</p><p>Ask and Remember </p></td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Select the Usage mode to create allocation relationships between an Action and Part Property representing a swimlane in which the action is contained. Select the Definition mode to create an allocation relationship between a behavior of action (Activity) and Block representing a swimlane in which the action is contained.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Item Flow Creation Mode</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">Direct</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;"><p>Between Part Types</p><p>Direct</p></td><td style="width: 55.98%;"><p>Select the mode for managing Item Flow in your project:</p><p>Select &quot;Direct&quot; to create the Item Flow between ports or parts in SysML Internal Block Diagram.</p><p>Select &quot;Between Part Types&quot; to create the Item Flow between Part property types. The &quot;Between Part Types&quot; option also allows you to quickly realize and synchronize Flows on Activity Edges, Connectors, and Messages. </p></td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Remove Not Realized Item Flows</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">false</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">true/false</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Set to true to automatically remove Item Flows after the last element that realizes them is deleted. </td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Creation of Contextual Relationships</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">Disabled</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;"><p>Fully enabled</p><p>Partially Enabled</p><p>Disabled</p></td><td style="width: 55.98%;"><p>Select &quot;Fully Enabled&quot; to establish relationships contextualization throughout the project.</p><p>Select &quot;Partially Enabled&quot; to ignore the contextualization while creating relationships on the diagram pane.</p><p>Select &quot;Disabled&quot; to bypass contextualization.</p></td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Show Item Flows on Affected Diagrams</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">true</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">true/false</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Set to true to update all affected diagrams and show the Conveyed Items of Item Flows realized on Connectors, Activity Edges, Messages, or Associations whenever a new Item Flow is created or an existing one is modified.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Check Item Flow Realization Compatibility</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">true</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">true/false</td><td style="width: 55.98%;"><p>Set to true to check the Item Flow compatibility with the behavior model while running the Behavior to Structure Synchronization. <br />Learn more about <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12PTWRT" ri:content-title="UAF Behavior to structure synchronization" /></ac:link>.</p><p>Learn more about <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="SYSMLPTWRT" ri:content-title="Behavior to structure synchronization" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SysML Behavior to structure synchronization]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p></td></tr><tr><td>Generate Diagram Layout Annotations</td><td>true</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">true/false</td><td style="width: 55.98%;"><p>Set the default option for generating diagram layout annotations for the Modelica export.</p></td></tr><tr><td class="highlight-grey" colspan="4" data-highlight-colour="grey" style="width: 100.0%;" title="Background colour :">UAF</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Architecture Framework</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">-</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">DoDAF, DoDAF 2.0, MODAF, NAF, NAF 4.0, UAF<br />UAF Enterprise Architecture</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">The architecture framework option identifies the environment and a subset of aliases to use within the context of the project (i.e., UAF, DoDAF, MODAF, etc.). To change the framework, go to File &gt; Convert To.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">ISO Date Time format</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">YYYY-MM-DD</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;"><p>Various, according to your locale</p></td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Select ISO Date Time format for setting dates for elements.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Use MIL-STD-2525 Symbology Library</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">None</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">Available library versions</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Specifies which MIL-STD-2525 Symbology Library Version to use for the project.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">BPMN Compliance Mode</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">false</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">true/false</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Specifies whether the compliant BPMN stereotypes should be applied to UAF Elements. The options must be true to create UAF diagrams dependent on the Business Process Diagram.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Map UAF Information Elements on ERD Entities</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">false</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">true/false</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Specifies whether the Entity stereotype should be applied to the UAF Entity Item element. The options must be true to create a UAF Entity Relationship Diagram.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Auto Check Model Completeness</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">false</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">true/false</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">If true, the user will be automatically notified about model incompleteness (recommended for novice UAF users). Note, that the auto-check may decrease the performance of the modeling tool. </td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Respect UAF Information Flows on Generalization</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">true</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">true/false</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Specifies whether the realization of inherited Operational Exchanges/Resource Exchanges is allowed or not. </td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Respect UAF Information Flows on Aggregation</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">true</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">true/false</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Specifies whether the realization of aggregated Operational Exchanges/Resource Exchanges is allowed or not. </td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Default Property Sets owner</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">-</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">-</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Defines the default owner for Property Sets, that are created using Property Set Models such as SV-7, NSV-7, or SvcV-7.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Inform about user read-only projects after conversion</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">true</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">true/false</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Set to true to inform about user read-only projects that haven't been converted during project conversion.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Show default documentation for UAF Packages</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">true</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">true/false</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Specifies whether the default Package documentation should be shown in the UAF Packages Documentation property:<br />Set to true to show the default Package documentation for the newly created UAF Packages.<br />Set to false to create the new UAF Packages without the default documentation. <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12PTWRT" ri:content-title="Show default documentation for UAF packages" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more>>>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;"><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">Sub-resources depth in Strategic Phasing Gantt Chart</span></td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">3</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">1-10</td><td style="width: 55.98%;"><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">Set the level of sub-resources hierarchy displayed in the Capability Provisions panel of the Strategic Phasing, CV-3, StV-3, NCV-3, or Cr Gantt Chart.</span></td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Default Operational Constraints Owner</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">Operational Constraints </td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">-</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Defines the default owner for Operational constraints. Depending on the framework, the default owner is Operational Constraints, Business Rules, OV-6a, NOV-6a, or L8. Change the default owner by choosing a different one from the model.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Default Resource Constraints Owner</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">Resource Constraints </td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">-</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Defines the default owner for Resource constraints. Depending on the framework, the default owner is Resource Constraints, SV-10a, NSV-10a, or P8. Change the default owner by choosing a different one from the model.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Default Service Constraints Owner</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">Service Constraints </td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">-</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Defines the default owner for Service Policies and Service Contracts constraints. Depending on the framework, the default owner for the Service Policies constraints is Service Constraints, SOV-4a, SvcV-10a, or S8 and for the Service Contracts, the default owner is Service Constraints. Change the default owner by choosing a different one from the model.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Default Strategic Constraints Owner</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">Strategic Constraints </td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">-</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Defines the default owner for Strategic constraints. Depending on the framework, the default owner is Strategic Constraints or C8. Change the default owner by choosing a different one from the model.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Default Security Constraints Owner</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;"> Security Constraints </td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">-</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Defines the default owner for Security constraints. Depending on the framework, the default owner is Security Constraints. Change the default owner by choosing a different one from the model.</td></tr><tr><td class="highlight-#f4f5f7" colspan="4" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7" style="width: 100.0%;" title="Background color : Light grey 100%">Validation</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Validation Scope</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">-</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">multi-selection</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Specify the validation scope. The validation scope can be the entire model or some part of it. When the validation is run, each validation rule is evaluated for each suitable element in the validation scope.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Validate only Visible Diagrams</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">true</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">true/false</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Set to true to validate only elements in one or more visible diagrams.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Exclude Elements from Used Read-Only Project</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">true</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">true/false</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Set to true to exclude projects used in read-only mode and the elements that exist in these projects. Excluded projects will not be validated.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Mark in Tree and Diagrams</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">true</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">true/false</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Set to true to highlight the invalid model elements in the browser with a small x symbol. The element symbol will be colored according to the severity level of the problem in the diagram.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Ignored Validation Suites</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">-</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">multi-selection</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Specify the validation suites which should not be run during the model validation.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Ignored Validation Rules</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">-</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">multi-selection</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Specify the validation rules that should not be run during the automatic model validation.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Minimal Severity</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">warning</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;"><p>debug</p><p>info</p><p>warning</p><p>error</p><p>fatal</p></td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Specify the minimal severity. When the validation is running, only the validation rules of the specified and the higher severity level will be run.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Composition Inspection</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">Standard</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;"><p>Standard</p><p>Advanced</p></td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Select the level of composition inspection in your projects. The Standard inspection searches for issues only in the project, while the Advanced inspection, in addition to the Standard inspection, searches for issues in all the used projects. The inspection runs validation rules which validate the correctness of model integrity and project composition.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Detect Illegal Model References in Shared Projects</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">true</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">true/false</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Set to true to check for illegal model references in projects. Illegal references are one of the main reasons why recovered elements appear in shared projects.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Ignored Validation Suites</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">-</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">multi-selection</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Specify the validation suites which should not be run during the passive diagram validation.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Ignored Validation Rules</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">-</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">multi-selection</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Specify the validation rules which should not be run during the passive diagram validation.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Minimal Severity</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">warning</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;"><p>debug</p><p>info</p><p>warning</p><p>error</p><p>fatal</p></td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Specify the minimal severity. When passive diagram validation is running, only the validation rules of the specified and the higher severity level will be run.</td></tr><tr><td class="highlight-#deebff" colspan="4" data-highlight-colour="#deebff" style="width: 100.0%;" title="Background color : Light blue 35%"><strong>Diagram Info</strong></td></tr><tr><td colspan="4" style="width: 100.0%;">Diagram information shows summarized data in each diagram, e.g. the author, creation and modification dates, etc. Choose the diagram tags in standard mode, their values should be shown in a predefined diagram information table, or choose how diagram information should e represented using the HTML code.</td></tr><tr><td class="highlight-#deebff" colspan="4" data-highlight-colour="#deebff" style="width: 100.0%;" title="Background color : Light blue 35%"><strong>Symbol styles</strong></td></tr><tr><td colspan="4" style="width: 100.0%;">Specify the shape, path, diagram, and stereotype symbol properties. Create, edit, clone, import/export, or remove element display styles. Also, set default styles or apply new styles to existing diagrams.</td></tr><tr><td class="highlight-#f4f5f7" colspan="4" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7" style="width: 100.0%;" title="Background color : Light grey 100%">Default</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Fill Color</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">RGB [255, 255, 204]</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">Custom</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Change the fill color of the shape.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Use Fill Color</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">true</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">true/false</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Shapes are colored on the diagram.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Pen Color</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">RGB [66, 66, 66]</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">Custom</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Change the color of the path or lines around shapes.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Text Color</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">RGB [0, 0, 0]</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">Custom</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Change the color of the text.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Line Width</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">1</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">Real</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Specify the line width of the symbol.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Font</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">Arial 11</td><td 10.3634%;"><p>Unspecified</p><p>1:1</p><p>4:3</p><p>3:4</p><p>16:9</p><p>9:16</p><p>A4 Landscape</p><p>A4 Portrait</p><p>US Letter Landscape</p><p>US Letter Portrait</p></td><td style="width: Corners</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">true</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">true/false</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Use rounded corners on the diagram frame. Diagram frames in Activity and State Machine diagrams can be represented as rounded rectangles.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Suspend Auto-Resizing of Shapes</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">Same as Diagram</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;"><p>Same as Diagram</p><p>true</p><p>false</p></td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Set to true to keep the original size of shapes while editing the model by cutting text to fit inside the shape. You can use the Resize to Fit Content command (a small black square on the bottom line of the selected shape) to adjust the size of the shape to fit the content completely.</td></tr><tr><td class="highlight-#f4f5f7" colspan="4" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7" style="width: 100.0%;" title="Background color : Light grey 100%">Stereotypes</td></tr><tr><td colspan="4" style="width: 100.0%;">Create elements' properties by stereotype. These properties will be applied when the symbol of the stereotyped element is created on the diagram pane.</td></tr><tr><td class="highlight-#deebff" colspan="4" data-highlight-colour="#deebff" style="width: 100.0%;" title="Background color : Light blue 35%"><strong>Default model properties</strong></td></tr><tr><td colspan="4" style="width: 100.0%;">In the Default model properties tab, select an element and change its default property values (the element will have these values after its creation). To change the default value only for a specific diagram, go to Diagrams main menu / Customize and edit the selected element properties. </td></tr><tr><td class="highlight-#deebff" colspan="4" data-highlight-colour="#deebff" style="width: 100.0%;" title="Background color : Light blue 35%"><strong>Code Engineering</strong></td></tr><tr><td colspan="4" style="width: 100.0%;"><p>Set general code generation or code reversing options. Also, configure generated code formatting styles for the selected programming languages. Subcategories:</p><p>Code Generation</p><p>Reverse</p><p>Java Language Options</p><p>C++ Language Options</p><p>CORBA IDL 3.0 Language Options</p></td></tr></tbody></table><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304009299 space=VTO version=1 -->
## PAGE 00032: (2026x Refresh1) Switching between projects with different AF related perspectives

- page_id: `304009299`
- space_key: `VTO`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/VTO/pages/304009299/2026x+Refresh1+Switching+between+projects+with+different+AF+related+perspectives
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-29T15:24:16.175+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Virtual Twin of the Organization Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Virtual Twin of the Organization Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) User Guide > (2026x Refresh1) Getting started > (2026x Refresh1) Customizing and Selecting Perspective
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A particular behavior is applied when switching between projects with different Architecture Framework (AF) related perspectives.When you work with two projects using different perspectives than those projects were created with and switch between them, a message is displayed.You can choose whether to change the perspective according to the project's framework or the latest used perspective in that message.

In the following table, see the cases of the actions required when switching between projects A and B.

| Actions of Project A | Project A perspective according to AF | The last used perspective of Project A | Actions of Project B | Project B perspective according to AF | The last used perspective of Project B | Required actions |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Create Project A | UAF | - | Create project B | MODAF | - | The project you are opening/creating requires the MODAF perspective. The perspective will be changed to MODAF automatically. (In the example below, Case #1) |
| Open Project A | UAF | - | Open project B | MODAF | - | The project you are opening/creating requires the MODAF perspective. The perspective will be changed to MODAF automatically. (In the example below, Case #1) |
| Migrate/Open/Create Project A | UAF | - | Migrate project B | MODAF | - | The project you are opening/creating requires the MODAF perspective. The perspective will be changed to MODAF automatically.(In the example below, Case #1) |
| Create Project A | UAF | - | Create project B | UAF | - | No actions |
| Open project A | UAF | - | Open project B | UAF | - | No actions |
| Migrate/Open/Create Project A | UAF | - | Migrate project B | UAF | - | No actions |
| Create Project A | UAF | MODAF | Create project B | MODAF | - | No actions |
| Open Project A | UAF | MODAF | Open project B | MODAF | - | No actions |
| Switch to opened Project A | UAF | MODAF | Switch to opened project B | UAF | - | The project you are opening/creating requires the UAF perspective. The perspective will be changed to UAF automatically.(In the example below, Case #1) |
| Switch to opened Project A | UAF | - | Switch to opened project B | NAF | - | The project you are opening/creating requires the NAF perspective. The perspective will be changed to NAF automatically.(In the example below, Case #1) |
| Switch to opened Project A | UAF | MODAF | Switch to opened project B | NAF | DoDAF 2 | The project you are opening requires the NAF perspective.To continue, choose one of the following:OK to switch the perspective to NAF (according to the framework).Cancel to switch the perspective to DoDAF 2(the latest used perspective).(In the example below, Case #2) |
| Switch to opened Project A | UAF | MODAF | Switch to opened project B | MODAF | DoDAF 2 | The project you are opening requires the MODAF perspective.To continue, choose one of the following:OK to switch the perspective to MODAF (according to the framework).Cancel to switch the perspective to DoDAF 2 (the latest used perspective).(In the example below, Case #2) |

##### Examples of messages

Here are examples of messages you receive when switching projects with different perspectives.

**Case #1.**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

**Case #2.**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=304009303 space=VTO version=1 -->
## PAGE 00033: (2026x Refresh1) Toolbars

- page_id: `304009303`
- space_key: `VTO`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/VTO/pages/304009303/2026x+Refresh1+Toolbars
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2026-04-29T15:24:16.430+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Virtual Twin of the Organization Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) Virtual Twin of the Organization Documentation > (2026x Refresh1) User Guide > (2026x Refresh1) Getting started > (2026x Refresh1) Understanding the user interface
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Toolbars help to speed up your work when performing commonly used tasks. Your modeling tool has two types of toolbars:

- Main toolbars - contain commands for basic project-related tasks and are located at the top of the modeling tool window, right under the main menu.
- Diagram toolbars - contain commands for working with diagram symbols and are located at the top of the diagram pane.

To show or hide different toolbars

- In the main menu, click View , select either Main Toolbars or Diagram Toolbars , and in the displayed menu select/clear the check boxes of the toolbars that you want to show/hide.
- Right-click the desired toolbar area and in the shortcut menu select/clear the check boxes of the toolbars that you want to show/hide.

##### Customizing toolbars

You can customize existing toolbars by adding or removing buttons and also create new custom toolbars.

To add a new toolbar

1. Do one of the following:
  - In the main menu, select View > Main Toolbars / Diagram Toolbars > Customize .
  - Right-click the toolbar area, and in the shortcut menu select Customize .
2. In the Customize Toolbars dialog, select the desired toolbar group and click the Add > New Toolbar .
3. Enter the name of the new toolbar and click OK .

After completing the above steps, a new toolbar is created in the selected toolbar group, and you can choose to show/hide it in the shortcut menu of the respective toolbar area. Note that the newly created toolbar does not have any buttons, so you need to add them after creating it.

To add a new button to a toolbar

1. Do one of the following:
  - In the main menu, select View > Main Toolbars / Diagram Toolbars > Customize .
  - Right-click the toolbar area, and in the shortcut menu select Customize .
2. In the Customize Toolbars dialog, select the desired toolbar and click the Add > Button . [ATTACHMENT filename='customize_toolbars_dialog.png']
3. In the Add Button dialog, select the desired button or command and click OK .
4. Click OK one more time to close the Customize Toolbars dialog.

A new button is added to the selected toolbar. To add more than one button, repeat steps 2 and 3 as many times as you need.

To remove a toolbar or button

1. Do one of the following:
  - In the main menu, select View > Main Toolbars / Diagram Toolbars > Customize .
  - Right-click the toolbar area, and in the shortcut menu select Customize .
2. In the Customize Toolbars dialog, select the toolbar or button you want to remove and click the Remove button.

After completing the above steps, the selected toolbar or button is removed from the respective toolbar area or toolbar. Toolbars that have been removed do not appear in the menu for selecting which toolbars you want to show.

#### NOTE: Restoring default toolbars

Restoring default toolbars

To undo all the actions for customizing toolbars, open the **Customize Toolbars** dialog, as described in step 1 above, and click the **Reset to Defaults** button.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>Toolbars help to speed up your work when performing commonly used tasks. Your modeling tool has two types of toolbars:</p><ul><li>Main toolbars - contain commands for basic project-related tasks and are located at the top of the modeling tool window, right under the main menu.</li><li>Diagram toolbars - contain commands for working with diagram symbols and are located at the top of the diagram pane.</li></ul><p><br /></p><p>To show or hide different toolbars</p><hr /><ul><li>In the main menu, click <strong>View</strong>, select either <strong>Main Toolbars</strong> or <strong>Diagram Toolbars</strong>, and in the displayed menu<strong> </strong>select/clear the check boxes of the toolbars that you want to show/hide.</li><li>Right-click the desired toolbar area and in the shortcut menu select/clear the check boxes of the toolbars that you want to show/hide.</li></ul><h3>Customizing toolbars</h3><p>You can customize existing toolbars by adding or removing buttons and also create new custom toolbars.</p><p><br /></p><p>To add a new toolbar</p><hr /><ol><li>Do one of the following:<ul><li>In the main menu, select <strong>View</strong> &gt; <strong>Main Toolbars</strong>/<strong>Diagram Toolbars</strong> &gt; <strong>Customize</strong>.</li><li>Right-click the toolbar area, and in the shortcut menu select <strong>Customize</strong>.</li></ul></li><li>In the <strong>Customize Toolbars</strong> dialog, select the desired toolbar group and click the <strong>Add</strong> &gt;<strong> New Toolbar</strong>.</li><li>Enter the name of the new toolbar and click <strong>OK</strong>. </li></ol><p><br />After completing the above steps, a new toolbar is created in the selected toolbar group, and you can choose to show/hide it in the shortcut menu of the respective toolbar area. To add more than one button, repeat steps 2 and 3 as many times as you need.</p><p><br /></p><p>To remove a toolbar or button</p><hr /><ol><li>Do one of the following:<ul><li>In the main menu, select <strong>View</strong> &gt; <strong>Main Toolbars</strong>/<strong>Diagram Toolbars</strong> &gt; <strong>Customize</strong>.</li><li>Right-click the toolbar area, and in the shortcut menu select <strong>Customize</strong>.</li></ul></li><li>In the <strong>Customize Toolbars</strong> dialog, select the toolbar or button you want to remove and click the <strong>Remove</strong> button.</li></ol><p><br />After completing the above steps, the selected toolbar or button is removed from the respective toolbar area or toolbar. Toolbars that have been removed do not appear in the menu for selecting which toolbars you want to show.</p><p><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="67428948-3ddd-4460-9978-eac77bf76b73"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Restoring default toolbars</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>To undo all the actions for customizing toolbars, open the <strong>Customize Toolbars</strong> dialog, as described in step 1 above, and click the <strong>Reset to Defaults</strong> button.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````
