contents of the selected element in a new tab of the Model Browser. The selected element becomes the root node of the new tree.</p><p>You can choose to open elements in:</p><ul><li>New Containment Tree</li><li>New Structure Tree</li><li>New Inheritance Tree</li></ul><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="inheritance_tab.png" /></ac:image></p></div></td></tr><tr><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><ac:image ac:height="17" ac:width="18"><ri:attachment ri:filename="favorite_button.png" /></ac:image></p></div></td><td><p>Favorites</p></td><td><p>Opens the Favorites menu for choosing to do one of the following:</p><ul><li>Add a selected element to favorites</li><li>Open the dialog for managing favorites<span style="color: rgb(129,137,156);"> </span></li><li>Navigate to a desired favorite in the Containment tree</li></ul></td></tr><tr><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><ac:image ac:height="19" ac:width="19"><ri:attachment ri:filename="quick_find_button.png" /></ac:image></p></div></td><td><p>Quick Find</p></td><td><p>Opens the <strong>Quick Find</strong> dialog.</p></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><div class="content-wrapper"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="options_button.png" /></ac:image></div></td><td colspan="1">Options</td><td colspan="1"><p>Click to open the Options menu and then select:</p><ul><li><strong><strong>Invert Tree</strong> </strong>- inverts the tree hierarchy - shows the child<br />element at the top of the hierarchy.<strong><br /></strong></li><li><strong>Show </strong><strong>Applied Stereotypes</strong> - shows or hides the applied <br />stereotypes next to the element name in the Containment tree.</li></ul></td></tr></tbody></table><h3>Working with model elements in the Inheritance tree</h3><p><br /></p><p>You can work with elements in the Inheritance tree using the commands available from the selected element's shortcut menu. The shortcut menu contains the following commands:</p><table class="wrapped relative-table" style="width: 39.6427%;"><colgroup><col style="width: 28.0677%;" /><col style="width: 71.9323%;" /></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Command</strong></p></td><td><p><strong>Function</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Select in Containment</strong> <strong>Tree</strong></p></td><td><p>Opens the Containment tab and selects the <br />current element in the Containment tree.</p></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><strong>Select in Structure Tree</strong></td><td colspan="1"><div class="content-wrapper"><p>Opens the Structure tab and the structured classifier is selected.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="af49f33a-5b79-474a-834d-d8ee5e6a182b"><ac:rich-text-body><p>This command is available for structured classifiers (SysML).</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=258048744 space=VTO version=1 -->
## PAGE 00062: (2026x) Lock View tab

- page_id: `258048744`
- space_key: `VTO`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/VTO/pages/258048744/2026x+Lock+View+tab
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-24T15:42:09.727+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Virtual Twin of the Organization Documentation > (2026x) Virtual Twin of the Organization Documentation > (2026x) User Guide > (2026x) Getting started > (2026x) Understanding the user interface > (2026x) Model Browser
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The Lock View tab is displayed only when working with [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Collaborative modeling' space='MDTWRT'].

The Lock View tab is used to display what was locked in the project (locked elements, project structure, symbol styles) and by which users. Next to the locked element, you can see the name of the user who has locked it. Using the **Filter by user**list in the Lock View tab toolbar, you can see the elements, locked by the particular user.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To open the Lock View tab, do one of the following

- At the top of the Model Browser, click the Lock View tab.
- If the Containment tab is hidden, from the Window menu, select Lock View .

The Locked Elements tab consists of the following two components:

- Lock View tab toolbar
- Lock View tree

##### Lock View tab toolbar

The Lock View tab toolbar contains the following:

- Update Lock Information button
- Filter by user list
- Collapse All button
- Collapse Selected Recursively button

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

Using the **Filter by user** list you can select to display only the elements locked by the particular user.

##### Working with model elements in the Lock View tree

You can work with elements in the Lock View tree using the commands available from the selected element's shortcut menu. The shortcut menu contains the following commands:

| Command | Function |
| --- | --- |
| Lock | Locks or unlocks the particular element for edit. |
| Select in Containment Tree | Opens the Containment tab and selects the current element in the Containment tree. |
| Select in Inheritance Tree | Opens the Inheritance tab and selects the current element in the Inheritance tree. |

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=258048803 space=VTO version=1 -->
## PAGE 00063: (2026x) Main menu

- page_id: `258048803`
- space_key: `VTO`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/VTO/pages/258048803/2026x+Main+menu
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-24T15:42:15.503+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Virtual Twin of the Organization Documentation > (2026x) Virtual Twin of the Organization Documentation > (2026x) User Guide > (2026x) Getting started > (2026x) Understanding the user interface
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The main menu located at the top of the modeling tool window contains the following elements:

| Menu element | Description |
| --- | --- |
| File | Contains basic project management-related commands, e.g., , , and . Among other actions, this menu also allows you to import data from external sources, export a project, or . |
| Edit | Use this menu when working with diagrams on the Diagram pane. The Edit menu contains the commands for selecting, symbols or entire diagrams, and finding elements in the current project. |
| View | Contains commands allowing you to of a diagram, use and customize a , manage diagram tabs (if you have several open diagrams) and of your modeling tool. |
| Layout | Use this menu for managing the of the symbols on an active diagram. To enable certain Layout menu commands, you must select more than one symbol. |
| Diagrams | Allows you to , access diagram wizards and history, and . |
| Options | Use this menu to specify and , select or , and choose the style for your modeling tool. |
| Tools | Contains the set of commands allowing you to execute for converting models between different modeling domains, , on the actual testing model, generate code for the selected model data, create and organize , access the , and more. |
| Analyze | Use this menu to access the, create implementation matrices, analyze and show model dependencies, , , or , and perform other model analysis-related actions. |
| Collaborate | Contains the commands for . |
| Window | Allows you to manage the layout of different windows in your modeling tool. |
| Help | Among other actions, this menu allows opening online documentation, managing resources, and accessing modeling tool-related information. |

#### NOTE: Customizable menus

Customizable menus

You can choose which of these menus are shown/hidden by [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Customizing Perspectives' space='MDTWRT'].

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=258048683 space=VTO version=1 -->
## PAGE 00064: (2026x) Model Browser

- page_id: `258048683`
- space_key: `VTO`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/VTO/pages/258048683/2026x+Model+Browser
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-24T15:42:07.519+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Virtual Twin of the Organization Documentation > (2026x) Virtual Twin of the Organization Documentation > (2026x) User Guide > (2026x) Getting started > (2026x) Understanding the user interface
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Model Browser provides a visual representation of the hierarchy of your model elements.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The items in the Model Browser hierarchy can be:

- Compressed – a [IMAGE alt='' src=''] plus sign next to an icon indicates that the icon is compressed. This is the default setting. It means that the element contains other model elements. Click the plus sign to expand the icon and view its subordinate items.
- Expanded – a [IMAGE alt='' src=''] minus sign next to an icon indicates that the icon is fully expanded. Click the minus sign to collapse the item.

If there is no plus or minus sign next to an icon, it does not contain other model elements.

The Model Browser is a hierarchical navigation tool that allows you to manage your model data, including packages, components, classes, all UML diagrams, extension mechanisms, and other data. The Model Browser may be used as an alternative tool to the menus and toolbars. It is easier to work with project diagrams and data elements using the Model Browser.

You can perform the following operations in the Model Browser:

- Create and specify model elements.
- Copy, cut, and paste model elements.
- Delete model elements.
- Drag model elements to the Diagram pane and inside the Model Browser.
- Drag data in the Code engineering sets (you may create data in the root package *Model* branch, drag it to the Code Engineering sets, and then the round trip object is created automatically).
- View the hierarchy of all model elements.
- Create a symbol for the selected model element in the current diagram.
- Manage diagrams.
- Manage extension mechanisms, such as constraints, stereotypes, and tagged values.
- Java reverse of a class from the classpath.
- Adjust the code engineering sets.
- Generate code for particular sets.
- Filter visible items by any model type (for example, class, package, operation, component, state, and others - for both views and dates).
- Sort the visible items of the selected model element.
- Sort all model elements.
- Display search results.
- Display locked elements in the server project.

The Model Browser window has the following tabs**:**

- **Containment** tab groups data into logical sets.
- **Structure** tab represents deeply nested structures of thestructuredclassifier.
- **Inheritance** tab represents the model hierarchy of the project.
- **Diagrams** tab groups diagrams according to the diagrams type or shows them as a list.
- **Model Extensions** tab represents all predefined and created constraints and stereotypes.
- **Search Results** tab displays search results.

The **Search Results** tab is not displayed by default. It appears after you perform the search.

- **Lock View**tab represents locked elements of the server project.

Lock View

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

The Model Browser may be used as an alternative tool to the menus and toolbars. It is easier to work with project diagrams and data elements using the Model Browser.</span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">You can perform the following operations in the Model Browser:</span></p><ul><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Create and specify model elements.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Copy, cut, and paste model elements.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Delete model elements.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Drag model elements to the Diagram pane and inside the Model Browser. </span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Drag data in the Code engineering sets (you may create data in the root package <em>Model</em> branch, drag it to the Code Engineering sets, and then the round trip object is created automatically).</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">View the hierarchy of all model elements.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Create a symbol for the selected model element in the current diagram.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Manage diagrams.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Manage extension mechanisms, such as constraints, stereotypes, and tagged values.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Java reverse of a class from the classpath.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Adjust the code engineering sets.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Generate code for particular sets.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Filter visible items by any model type (for example, class, package, operation, component, state, and others - for both views and dates). </span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Sort the visible items of the selected model element.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Sort all model elements.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Display search results.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Display locked elements in the server project.</span></li></ul><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><br /></span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">The Model Browser window <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="03c23de3-5bbd-492c-8c52-a7e598fccbba">has the following tabs</ac:inline-comment-marker><strong><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="03c23de3-5bbd-492c-8c52-a7e598fccbba">:</ac:inline-comment-marker></strong></span></p><ul><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><strong>Containment</strong> tab groups data into logical sets. </span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><strong>Structure</strong> tab represents <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">deeply nested structures of the </span>structured<span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"> classifier.</span></span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><strong>Inheritance</strong> tab represents the model hierarchy of the project. </span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><strong>Diagrams</strong> tab groups diagrams according to the diagrams type or shows them as a list. </span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><strong>Model Extensions</strong> tab represents all predefined and created constraints and stereotypes. </span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><strong>Search Results</strong> tab displays search results.</span></li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="442cf16c-0e01-49b1-8886-185e06b067e3"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">The <strong>Search Results</strong> tab is not displayed by default. It appears after you perform the search.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ul><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><span><strong>Lock View </strong>tab represents locked elements of the server project.</span></span></li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8fefc1e6-3cd3-43bb-8906-90045de73346"><ac:rich-text-body>The <strong>Lock View</strong> tab is not displayed by default. It can appear if you are working on a server project. For more information, see <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Locking model for edit" ri:space-key="MDTWRT" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Representation of locked elements]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=258048722 space=VTO version=1 -->
## PAGE 00065: (2026x) Model Extensions tab

- page_id: `258048722`
- space_key: `VTO`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/VTO/pages/258048722/2026x+Model+Extensions+tab
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-24T15:42:08.640+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Virtual Twin of the Organization Documentation > (2026x) Virtual Twin of the Organization Documentation > (2026x) User Guide > (2026x) Getting started > (2026x) Understanding the user interface > (2026x) Model Browser
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The Model Extensions tab contains all Stereotypes that are predefined and created manually in the project. In this tree, you can create, review, copy/paste, and delete extension mechanisms.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To open the Model Extensions tab, do one of the following

- At the top of the Model Browser, click the Model Extensions tab.
- If the Model Extensions tab is hidden, from the **Window** menu, select **Model Extensions** You can change some Model Extensions tab properties in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Customizing environment options' space='MDTWRT']**Environment Options** dialog in the **Browser** options group.

Model Extensions tab consists of the following two components:

- Model Extensions tab toolbar
- Model Extensions tree

##### Model Extensions tab toolbar

**Model Extensions** tab toolbar contains the following buttons:

| Button | Tooltip text | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
|  | Collapse All | Collapses all of the current elements in the Containment tree. |
|  | Collapse Selected Recursively | Collapses only selected node and all subnodes in that node. |
|  | Group by Profiles | Groups extensions by the profiles. |
|  | Group by Metaclasses | Groups extensions by the metaclasses. |
|  | Favorites | Opens the Favorites menu for choosing to do one of the following:Add a selected element to favoritesOpen the dialog for managing favoritesNavigate to a desired favorite in the Containment tree |
|  | Quick Find | Opens the Quick Find dialog. |

The same actions you may perform using the shortcut menu of the Model Extensions tree.

##### Working with model elements in the Model Extensions tree

You can work with elements in the Model Extension tree using the commands available from the selected element's shortcut menu.

For more information about commands available from the shortcut menu in the Model Browser, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with model elements in the Model Browser' space='MDTWRT'].

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=258048704 space=VTO version=1 -->
## PAGE 00066: (2026x) Most common shortcut keys

- page_id: `258048704`
- space_key: `VTO`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/VTO/pages/258048704/2026x+Most+common+shortcut+keys
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-24T15:42:08.020+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Virtual Twin of the Organization Documentation > (2026x) Virtual Twin of the Organization Documentation > (2026x) User Guide > (2026x) Getting started
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The following table outlines the most commonly used shortcut keys:

| What do you want to do? | Shortcut keys |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
|  | Windows | OS X |
| Open the Find dialog | Ctrl+Shift+F | Cmd+Shift+F |
| Open the Quick Find dialog | Ctrl+Alt+F | Cmd+Alt+F |
| Open the Find and Replace dialog | Ctrl+R | Cmd+R |
| Remove both symbols from an active diagram and element from the model | Ctrl+D | Cmd+D |
| Remove only a symbol from an active diagram, but not the model element | Delete | Del |
| Select all shapes on an active diagram | Ctrl+A | Cmd+A |
| Select an element in the Containment treeSwitch from List to Tree view in the Select Element dialog | Alt+B | Alt+B |
| Zoom in/out Model Browser | Ctrl+Mouse scroll wheelCtrl+Numpad +/- | Cmd+Mouse scroll wheelCmd+Numpad +/- |
| Add more symbols to selection | Shift+Left mouse button | Shift+Left mouse button |
| Select elements of the same type | Alt+Left mouse button | Alt+Left mouse button |
| Open a list of possible elements to assign when typing on a symbol | Ctrl+Spacebar | Ctrl+Spacebar |
| Add a new line in the compartment of a shape, for example, create an attribute for a class | Shift+Enter | Shift+Enter |
| Open element's Specification window | Enter | Enter |
| Open element's Symbol Properties dialog | Alt+Enter | Alt+Enter |
| Open the Report Wizard | Ctrl+Shift+G | Cmd+Shift+G |
| See the list of recently opened diagrams | F12 | – |
| Cut a selected item | Ctrl+X | Cmd+X |
| Copy a shape or text | Ctrl+C | Cmd+C |
| Copy a shape | Drag the shape to the empty place on the diagram, while holding down Ctrl | – |
| Paste | Ctrl+V | Cmd+V |
| Paste with New Element | Ctrl+E | Cmd+E |
| Save | Ctrl+S | Cmd+S |
| Commit changes to the server | Ctrl+K | Cmd+K |
| Undo | Ctrl+Z | Cmd+Z |
| Redo | Ctrl+Y | Cmd+Y |
| Print | Ctrl+P | Cmd+P |
| Assign a shortcut key to print a report | Alt+ number 1 to 9 | Alt+ number 1 to 9 |
| Turn on the Full Screen mode | F11 | – |
| Open a recently closed diagram | Ctrl+Shift+T | – |
| Paste a symbol style | Ctrl+Shift+V | Cmd+Shift+V |
| Reverse path direction | Draw the path while holding down Alt | Draw the path while holding down Alt |
| Reorder elements/properties in a list, tree, and Specification window | Ctrl+UpCtrl+Down | Alt+UpAlt+Down |
| Select a previous or next result in a tree | Shift+F3F3 | Shift+F3F3 |

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=258048756 space=VTO version=1 -->
## PAGE 00068: (2026x) Process Guide for VTO

- page_id: `258048756`
- space_key: `VTO`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/VTO/pages/258048756/2026x+Process+Guide+for+VTO
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-24T15:42:10.231+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Virtual Twin of the Organization Documentation > (2026x) Virtual Twin of the Organization Documentation > (2026x) User Guide > (2026x) Procedures
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

When selecting a **UAF Project for Virtual Twin of the Organization** template upon creating a project, the new project will include a Process guide.

The Process guide describes the different steps that must be performed to create a Virtual Twin of an Organization (VTO). It provides details on the data to be created by the Enterprise Architect, which enables populating the **VTO Data Perspectives** that will be made available on the 3DEXPERIENCE Platform.

The Process guide focuses on **3 UAF viewpoints** in which data should be created: **Personnel**, **Resources**and **Projects.**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>When selecting a <strong>UAF Project for Virtual Twin of the Organization</strong> template upon creating a project, the new project will include a Process guide. </p><p>The Process guide describes the different steps that must be performed to create a Virtual Twin of an Organization (VTO). It provides details on the data to be created by the Enterprise Architect, which enables populating the <strong>VTO Data Perspectives</strong> that will be made available on the 3DEXPERIENCE Platform.</p><p>The Process guide focuses on <strong>3 UAF viewpoints</strong> in which data should be created: <strong>Personnel</strong>, <strong>Resources </strong>and <strong>Projects.</strong></p><p><br /></p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="VTO_process_guide.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Process Guide for VTO" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=258048778 space=VTO version=1 -->
## PAGE 00069: (2026x) Properties tab

- page_id: `258048778`
- space_key: `VTO`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/VTO/pages/258048778/2026x+Properties+tab
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-24T15:42:14.439+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Virtual Twin of the Organization Documentation > (2026x) Virtual Twin of the Organization Documentation > (2026x) User Guide > (2026x) Getting started > (2026x) Understanding the user interface > (2026x) Model Browser
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The **Properties** tab at the bottom of the Model Browser allows you to quickly access basic information about a selected element or diagram.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### An example of the Properties tab in the Quick properties panel

#### TIP: Multiple selection

Multiple selection

- If multiple elements or symbols of different kinds are selected, only their common properties are displayed in the Properties tab.
- If more than 10 elements in the Containment tree or symbols on the diagram pane are selected, properties are displayed only by request. You need to click the link Show common properties for the selected items .

To open the **Properties** tab, do one of the following

- In the Model Browser, click the Properties tab.
- On the Window menu, click Properties .

The **Properties** tab includes the following tabs:

| Tab name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Element | Contains the main properties of the element's or diagram's specification. |
| Symbol | Contains the element symbol's properties. noteThis tab is available for symbols only. |
| Tags | Contains the tagged values of the selected element.When you click the Show Only Applied Stereotypes Tags button, only the tabs with values are listed. |
| Language properties | Contains the same data as the Language Properties property group in the element's Specification window.This tab is available only for the elements used for code generation, that is, class, attribute, and operation. |
| Traceability | Contains the same data as the Traceability property group in the element's Specification window. |

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The <strong>Properties</strong> tab at the bottom of the Model Browser allows you to quickly access basic information about a selected element or diagram.</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="properties_tab.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">An example of the Properties tab in the Quick properties panel</h6><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="71c9ccf1-27fa-4b69-8875-bc558a54d42c"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Multiple selection</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>If multiple elements or symbols of different kinds are selected, only their common properties are displayed in the <strong>Properties</strong> tab.</li><li>If more than 10 elements in the Containment tree or symbols on the diagram pane are selected, properties are displayed only by request. You need to click the link <strong>Show common properties for the selected items</strong>.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>To open the <strong>Properties</strong> tab, do one of the following</p><hr /><ul><li>In the Model Browser, click the <strong>Properties</strong> tab.</li><li>On the <strong>Window</strong> menu, click <strong>Properties</strong>.</li></ul><p><br /></p><p>The <strong>Properties</strong> tab includes the following tabs:</p><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><td class="highlight-grey" title="Background colour : Grey" data-highlight-colour="grey"><p title=""><strong>Tab name</strong></p></td><td class="highlight-grey" title="Background colour : Grey" data-highlight-colour="grey"><p title=""><strong>Description</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Element</strong></p></td><td><p>Contains the main properties of the element's or diagram's specification.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Symbol</strong></p></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p>Contains the element symbol's properties.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="478b0e30-66de-460f-8206-0972e18e66e8"><ac:parameter ac:name=""> note</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>This tab is available for symbols only.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></div></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><strong>Tags</strong></td><td colspan="1"><div class="content-wrapper"><p>Contains the tagged values of the selected element.</p><p>When you click the <strong>Show Only Applied Stereotypes Tags</strong> button, only the tabs with values are listed.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="show_only_applied.png" /></ac:image></p></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Language properties</strong></p></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p>Contains the same data as the <strong>Language Properties</strong> property group in the element's <strong>Specification</strong> window.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1ef14a6d-dc2f-4fe2-9e03-b9cd57973703"><ac:rich-text-body><p>This tab is available only for the elements used for code generation, that is, class, attribute, and operation. <strong><span><br /></span></strong></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Traceability</strong></p></td><td><p>Contains the same data as the <strong>Traceability</strong> property group in the element's <strong>Specification</strong> window.</p></td></tr></tbody></table>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=258048765 space=VTO version=1 -->
## PAGE 00070: (2026x) Reporting Issues

- page_id: `258048765`
- space_key: `VTO`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/VTO/pages/258048765/2026x+Reporting+Issues
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-24T15:42:10.525+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Virtual Twin of the Organization Documentation > (2026x) Virtual Twin of the Organization Documentation
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Need help? Send notifications of a problem, suggest improvements for our modeling tools, or ask about new features.

###### offlineReport an issue when the program is unresponsive

If you encounter an issue or the modeling tool becomes unresponsive, a separately executable tool is provided for analyzing the status of the process to aid in bug submission.In these situations, manually start the *submit_issue.exe* file (located in the *<modeling tool installation directory>\bin folder*).After you start *submit_issue.exe*, the**Report an Issue** dialog opens.

In this dialog, you can easily dump threads, memory heap, or memory histogram into files and provide those files when reporting an issue to the support team.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The Report an Issue dialog in the offline mode

First of all, select the specific instance of the modeling tool to dump reports from. Then, click one of the following:

- **Dump Threads to Log File** - threads will be dumped into a Log file.
- **Dump Memory Heap to File** - memory heap will be dumped to a new .hprof file.
- **Dump Memory Histogram to File** - a memory histogram will be dumped to a new .txt file. To use the **Dump Memory Histogram to File** functionality, the application must run with the JDK or JRE that comes with the installation.

###### View and Submit Internal Errors

Internal errors are displayed as notifications by default. The internal error does not necessarily mean damage to your data but can be a reason for unexpected tool behavior. We highly recommend submitting internal errors to No Magic for further investigation. We strongly recommend attaching the log file. An internal error message appears at the bottom of the modeling tool.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Example of Internal Error message in the modeling tool

To view internal errors

To view internal errors you must open the **Internal Errors** dialog, using one of the two methods outlined below:

- Click the View internal errors button in the Notification Window.
- Click the notification icon on the status line.

To submit an error

1. Open the Internal Errors dialog.
2. Click the Export To File button. [IMAGE alt='' src='']
3. Specify the name and location of the error log file and click Export .
4. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Support' space='NMDOC'] and attach the exported error log file.

Internal errors will not be shown for 24 hours (or until you restart your modeling tool) after clicking the **Clear And Close** buttons.

To Display Internal Errors

1. From the Options menu, select Environment . The Environment options dialog will open.
2. In the General options group , click Internal Errors.
3. In the Internal Errors options list change the Display Internal Errors option value to true to display internal errors.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Need help? Send notifications of a problem, suggest improvements for our modeling tools, or ask about new features. </span></p><h4><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5bb4ad24-062c-4a01-8ff1-2f0000496544"><ac:parameter ac:name="">offline</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Report an issue when the program is unresponsive</h4><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">If you encounter an issue or the modeling tool becomes unresponsive, a separately executable tool is provided for analyzing the status of the process to aid in bug submission. </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">In these situations, manually start the <em><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">submit_issue.exe</span></em> file (located in the <em>&lt;modeling tool installation directory&gt;\bin folder</em>). </span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">After you start <em><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">submit_issue.exe</span></em>, the </span><strong>Report an Issue</strong> dialog <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">opens. </span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">In this dialog, you can easily dump threads, memory heap, or memory histogram into files and provide those files when reporting an issue to the support team.</span></p><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="report_an_issue_offline_dialog.png" /></ac:image></span></p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">The Report an Issue dialog in the offline mode</span></h6><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">First of all, select the specific instance of the modeling tool to dump reports from. Then, click one of the following:</span></p><ul><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><strong>Dump Threads to Log File</strong> - threads will be dumped into a Log file.</span></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><strong>Dump Memory Heap to File</strong> - memory heap will be dumped to a new .hprof file.</span></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><strong>Dump Memory Histogram to File</strong> - a memory histogram will be dumped to a new .txt file. <br /></span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="fd25920a-a29b-47bd-815e-fca56b11154a"><ac:rich-text-body><p>To use the <strong>Dump Memory Histogram to File</strong> functionality, the application must run with the JDK or JRE that comes with the installation.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li></ul><h4>View and Submit Internal Errors</h4><p>Internal errors are displayed as notifications by default. The internal error does not necessarily mean damage to your data but can be a reason for unexpected tool behavior. We highly recommend submitting internal errors to No Magic for further investigation. We strongly recommend attaching the log file. An internal error message appears at the bottom of the modeling tool. </p><p style="text-align: center;"><span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="internal_error_message_.png" /></ac:image></span></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Example of Internal Error message in the modeling tool</h6><p>To view internal errors</p><hr /><p>To view internal errors you must open the <strong>Internal Errors</strong> dialog, using one of the two methods outlined below:</p><ul><li>Click the <strong>View</strong> <strong>internal errors</strong> button in the Notification Window.</li><li>Click the <strong>notification</strong> icon on the status line.<br /><br /></li></ul><p>To submit an error</p><hr /><ol><li>Open the <strong>Internal Errors </strong>dialog.</li><li>Click the <strong>Export To File</strong> button.<br /><span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="internal_errors.png" /></ac:image></span></li><li>Specify the name and location of the error log file and click<strong> Export</strong>.</li><li class="ancestor-link parent-link"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="NMDOC" ri:content-title="Support" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Report an issue to the customer support]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> and attach the exported error log file. </li></ol><p>Internal errors will not be shown for 24 hours (or until you restart your modeling tool) after clicking the <strong>Clear And Close</strong> buttons.</p><p><br /></p><p>To Display Internal Errors</p><hr /><ol><li>From the <strong>Options</strong> menu, select <strong>Environment</strong>. The <strong>Environment options </strong>dialog will open.</li><li>In the <strong>General</strong> options group<strong>, </strong>click <strong>Internal Errors.</strong></li><li>In the <strong>Internal Errors</strong> options list change the <strong>Display Internal Errors</strong> option value to <em>true</em> to display internal errors.</li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=258048733 space=VTO version=1 -->
## PAGE 00071: (2026x) Search Results tab

- page_id: `258048733`
- space_key: `VTO`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/VTO/pages/258048733/2026x+Search+Results+tab
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-24T15:42:09.299+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Virtual Twin of the Organization Documentation > (2026x) Virtual Twin of the Organization Documentation > (2026x) User Guide > (2026x) Getting started > (2026x) Understanding the user interface > (2026x) Model Browser
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The Search Results tab is used to display search results. This tab opens only after [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Find dialog' space='MDTWRT'] by pressing Ctrl+Shift+F, or by clicking **Edit** > **Find**.

The tab is not displayed when performing a **[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Quick Find dialog' space='MDTWRT']**.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The **Search Results** tab consists of two components:

- 
- 

##### #searchresulttoolbarSearch Results tab toolbar

The Search Results tab toolbars contains the following buttons:

| Button | Tooltip text | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
|  | Collapse All | Collapses all of the current elements in the Containment tree. |
|  | Collapse Selected Recursively | Collapses only selected node and all subnodes in that node. |
|  | Find... | Opens the Find dialog. |
|  | Clear Results | Clears the results of the previous search. |
|  | Rerun Search | Reruns a search with the already specified search criteria. Search results are updated according to changes done in the model. |
|  | Save Search Results | Saves as a smart package either the search options or the search results. |
|  | Favorites | Opens the Favorites menu for choosing to do one of the following:Add a selected element to favoritesOpen the dialog for managing favoritesNavigate to a desired favorite in the Containment tree |
|  | Options | Click to open the Options menu and then select:Show Applied Stereotypes - shows or hides the applied stereotypes next to the element name in the Containment tree.Show Full Types - shows or hides the full types of typed elements (for example, operations, attributes, relationships, and other) in the Containment tree. |

The same actions you may perform using the shortcut menu of the Search Results tree. For more information about commands available from the shortcut menu in the Model Browser, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with model elements in the Model Browser' space='MDTWRT'].

##### #searchresulttreeWorking with model elements in the Search Results tree

The search results in the **Search Results** tree are displayed in the following two packages:

- Found in loaded diagrams – symbols that have been found in the diagram(s) are displayed in this package.
- Found in model – elements that have been found in the model data are displayed in this package.

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=258048792 space=VTO version=1 -->
## PAGE 00072: (2026x) Select Perspective dialog

- page_id: `258048792`
- space_key: `VTO`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/VTO/pages/258048792/2026x+Select+Perspective+dialog
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-24T15:42:15.046+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Virtual Twin of the Organization Documentation > (2026x) Virtual Twin of the Organization Documentation > (2026x) User Guide > (2026x) Getting started > (2026x) Customizing and Selecting Perspective
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

When you launch a modeling tool for the first time, the **Select Perspective**dialog appears with the ability to switch between perspectives.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

| Element Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Business Analyst | This perspective provides features for Business Analysts. The role is responsible for defining business architecture. Code engineering, transformations, extensions, and other non-related features are hidden. |
| Business Architect | This perspective provides features for Business Architects. The role is responsible for defining business architecture. Code engineering, transformations, extensions, and other non-related features are hidden. |
| DoDAF 2.0 Architect | This perspective provides features dedicated to DoDAF 2.0 Architects. UML modeling features, code engineering, transformations, and other non-DoDAF 2.0-related features are hidden. |
| DODAF Architect | This perspective provides features dedicated to DoDAF Architects. UML modeling features, code engineering, transformations, and other non-DoDAF-related features are hidden. |
| MODAF Architect | This perspective provides features dedicated to MODAF Architects. UML modeling features, code engineering, transformations, and other non-MODAF-related features are hidden. |
| NAF 4.0 Architect | This perspective provides features dedicated to NAF 4.0 Architects. UML modeling features, code engineering, transformations, and other non-NAF 4.0-related features are hidden. |
| NAF Architect | This perspective provides features dedicated to NAF Architects. UML modeling features, code engineering, transformations, and other non-NAF-related features are hidden. |
| SOA Engineer | This perspective is a set of roles primarily involved in designing and implementing projects: Software Architect, Designer, Interface Designer, and Database Designer. This is the default Magic Systems of Systems Architect and Cameo Enterprise Architect configuration. All functionalities are available in expert mode. |
| Software Architect | This perspective is dedicated to Software Architect, Designer, Interface Designer, and Database Designer roles that design and implement the project. All functionalities are available in expert mode. |
| System Analyst | This perspective is a set of roles primarily dedicated to eliciting requirements and modeling the system. Analysis features are highlighted. Code engineering, transformations, and other non-related features are hidden. |
| System Engineer | This perspective provides features dedicated to System Engineers. SysML support is accessible. Requirements elicitation and modeling features are highlighted. UML modeling features, code engineering, transformations, and other non-system-modeling-related features are hidden. |
| TOGAF Architect | This perspective provides features for TOGAF Architects. The role is responsible for defining business architecture. Code engineering, transformations, extensions, and other non-related features are hidden. |
| UAF Architect VTO | The perspective provides features dedicated to UAF Architects for creation of a Virtual Twin of the Organization. This perspective reduces the standard set of menus/commands show in non-expert mode. UML modeling features, code engineering, transformations, and other non-VTO-related features are hidden. |
| UAF Architect | The perspective provides features dedicated to UAF Architects with all the UAF products available. UML modeling features, code engineering, transformations, and other non-UAF-related features are hidden. |
| EA-BPMN Architect* | This perspective provides features dedicated to Enterprise and IT Architects with the essential elements for this area: capabilities, requirements, operational behaviors (with BPMN), resources (hardware, software, facilities), data, and personnel. The user interface is simplified; advanced UAF elements and diagrams are hidden. |
| Zachman Framework | This Quick Start perspective provides basic features dedicated to modeling and not an overpopulated interface for quick learning. Code engineering, transformations, and other advanced features are hidden but easily accessible in the expert mode of this perspective. |
| Customize | Open the Customize Perspectives dialog to customize the selected perspective. You can make your tool interface less complicated by hiding irrelevant features or commands. For example, you can group functional features, and show, or hide selected commands. |

*Starting with the 2024x version, the **UAF Enterprise Architecture Project**was renamed to**EA with BPMN Project.**

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

