15.8529%;">Check for Cyclic Dependencies among Used Projects</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">false</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">true/false</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Set to true to check for cyclic dependencies during the project export or package share. </td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Dependency Checker Severity Level</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">Info</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;"><p>Info</p><p>Warning</p><p>Error</p></td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Specify the dependency checker severity. The dependencies of the specified and higher severity will be displayed.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Ignore Standard/System Profiles</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">true</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">true/false</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Set to true to exclude standard/system profiles when checking for cyclic dependencies in used projects.</td></tr><tr><td class="highlight-#f4f5f7" colspan="4" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7" style="width: 100.0%;" title="Background color : Light grey 100%">Diagrams</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Use i18n property for text rendering in the diagrams</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">true</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">true/false</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Set to false to disable the i18n property. If the i18n property is set to false, all text symbols that are displayed in the diagram pane (for example, text which is displayed on the symbol) will not be distorted at any zoom ratio. </td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Display All Pins in Diagrams</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">true</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">true/false</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Set to true to always display pin elements in the diagrams. Note: if this option is set to true and the pin symbol is deleted from the diagram, then the corresponding pin element will be deleted from the model, too.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Display All Activity Parameter Nodes in Diagrams</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">true</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">true/false</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Set to true to always display activity parameter node elements in the diagrams. Note: if this option is set to true and the activity parameter node symbol is deleted from the diagram, then the corresponding activity parameter node element will be deleted from the model, too. </td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Use different Join/Fork and Decision/Merge notations</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">true</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">true/false</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Use different notations to draw Join/Fork and Decision/Merge.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Hide Information Flow If Conveyed Information is Missing</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">false</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">true/false</td><td style="width: 55.98%;"><p>Set to true to hide the arrow notation that adorns the realizing element if the Conveyed Information of a realized Information Flow is hidden or unspecified.</p><p><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12PTWRT" ri:content-title="Working with exchanges" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more >>>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p><p><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="SYSMLPTWRT" ri:content-title="Setting project options" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more >>>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p></td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Diagram Context</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">Create Automatically</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;"><p>Create Automatically</p><p>Do Not Create</p></td><td style="width: 55.98%;">A new diagram context element will be created automatically when the new diagram owned by a Package is created. This option is valid for all diagrams based on a Composite Structure Diagram.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Apply Image To</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">Type and Notify</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;"><p>Type and Notify</p><p>Type</p><p>Property</p></td><td style="width: 55.98%;">(This is personal property, meaning its value is unique for each user). Specify whether to apply an image to the property or its type when dragging or selecting an image from the Image Library or other resource. This option is available when the Type Selection Mode is switched on.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Use Aspects</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">false</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">true/false</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Set to true to use definable diagram aspects in the project.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Ignored Aspects</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">-</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">multi-selection</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Specify the aspects which should not be used in the project.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Suspend Auto-Resizing of Shapes</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">true</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">true/false</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Set to true to keep the original size of shapes while editing the model by cutting text to fit inside the shape. You can use the Resize to Fit Content command (a small black square on the bottom line of the selected shape) to adjust the size of the shape to fit the content completely.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Suspend Auto-Displaying of Labels</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">true</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">true/false</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Set to true if you do not want to automatically show labels on the diagram pane while editing the model. You can use the Show Suspended Labels command (a small black square on the selected path) to show suspended labels on the diagram pane.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Highlight Suspended Symbols</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">true</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">true/false</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Set to true to highlight suspended symbols with not completely visible content.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Time Limit to Build Diagram </td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">60</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">Real</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Specify the maximum time (in seconds) allotted for diagram building while exporting a diagram. If the time limit is exceeded, the diagram is not exported. Set to 0 to export the diagram without the time limit. </td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Image Height Limit</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">10000</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">Real</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Specify the maximum image height (in pixels) for diagram export. If the height limit is exceeded, the exported diagram is cropped, or the diagram is not exported at all, depending on the selected preference. Set to 0 to export the diagram without the image height limit. </td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Image Width Limit</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">10000</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">Real</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Specify the maximum image width (in pixels) for diagram export. If the width limit is exceeded, the exported diagram is cropped, or the diagram is not exported at all, depending on the selected preference. Set to 0 to export the diagram without the image width limit. </td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Export Preference When Limits Exceeded</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">Export Cropped Diagram</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;"><p>Export Cropped Diagram</p><p>Do Not Export</p></td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Specify the diagram export preference if the diagram image height and/or width limits are exceeded.</td></tr><tr><td class="highlight-#f4f5f7" colspan="4" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7" style="width: 100.0%;" title="Background color : Light grey 100%">Element References</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Default Element Hyperlink Text Display Mode</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">Name</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;"><p>Name</p><p>Icon + Representation text</p><p>Representation text</p><p>Custom text</p></td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Specify how the hyperlink to the model element is displayed by default. Select 'Name' to display the element name from its Specification window. The 'Representation text' displays the full element title, visible in the Containment tree. Choose the 'Icon+Representation text' to display the element icon together with the full element title. Select 'Custom text' to type your own text.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Default Element Hyperlink Text Update Mode</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">Automatically updated</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;"><p>Automatically updated</p><p>Automatically checked</p><p>Manually checked</p><p>Do not update</p></td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Specify when the hyperlink to the model element will be updated by default if the referenced element and the hyperlink become inconsistent. Select 'Automatically updated' to update hyperlink text or the referenced element name automatically. Choose 'Automatically checked' to enable active validation rules which check if the hyperlink and the referenced element are coherent. The 'Manually checked' option allows you to run validation manually, while the 'Do not update' option means that validation will not find any inconsistencies.</td></tr><tr><td class="highlight-#f4f5f7" colspan="4" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7" style="width: 100.0%;" title="Background color : Light grey 100%">General</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Path to Used Project</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;"><p>&lt;project.dir&gt;</p><p>&lt;install.root&gt;\profiles</p><p>&lt;install.root&gt;\modelLibraries</p></td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">Custom</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Specify the paths to store used projects and extension elements.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Enable Dot Notation for Associations</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">false</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">true/false</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Set to true to use the dot notation for associations. If the dot notation is applied to an association, the association end is decorated with the dot if that end is owned by a classifier. The absence of the dot signifies ownership by the association.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Change Ownership of Non-Navigable Association End when Changing Navigability</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">true</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">true/false</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Set to true to automatically change ownership of the non-navigable association end when the navigability of the association was changed.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Qualified Name Display Style</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">Model Library Relative</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;"><p>Absolute</p><p>Model Relative</p><p>Model Library Relative</p><p>Model or Model Library Relative</p></td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Specify the mode of displaying the element qualified name. If the Model Library Relative mode is specified, it means that the element's full qualified name hierarchy is displayed on the shape, starting from the </td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Synchronize Parameters and Arguments</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">true</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">true/false</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Set to true to synchronize the associated elements on elements creation or update.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Layout Template Creation Mode</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">Definition</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;"><p>Usage</p><p>Definition</p></td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Specify the mode layout template diagram creation. Choose 'Definition' to create the layout templates as a class diagram. Choose 'Usage' to create the layout templates as a composite structure diagram.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Layout Use Case Scenario Activity Diagram</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">false</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">true/false</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Set to true to automatically lay out the activity diagram of the use case scenario every time it is opened.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Decimal Places</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">4</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">Real</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Specify the maximum number of decimal places to be used when displaying the real number in tables and element Specification windows.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Eclipse UML2 XMI Output Location</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">-</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">Custom</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Specify the path to the location where the exported Eclipse UML2 XMI will be saved.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Use Glossary</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">true</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">true/false</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Set to true to use the glossary functionality: mark terms in the text, create terms directly from the text, or reuse them in newly created texts.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Show Excel/CSV Import Migration Message</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">true</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">true/false</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">If true, the Migration message is shown each time when importing Excel/CSV mapping created with the CSV Import plugin.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Tooltips Style</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">Do Not Show</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;"><p>Do Not Show</p><p>Show Element Name</p><p>Show Element Documentation</p></td><td style="width: 55.98%;">(This is personal property, meaning its value is unique for each user). Specify the mode to display the element tooltip.</td></tr><tr><td class="highlight-#f4f5f7" colspan="4" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7" style="width: 100.0%;" title="Background color : Light grey 100%">Legends</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Filtering Preference</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">Show</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;"><p>Show</p><p>Hide</p></td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Specify the default filtering preference for Legends.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Use Legends</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">true</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">true/false</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Set to true to enable Legends adorning and filtering functionalities across all tables and diagrams with symbols in the project.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Ignored Legends</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">-</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">multi-selection</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Specify the Legends which should not be used in the project.</td></tr><tr><td class="highlight-#f4f5f7" colspan="4" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7" style="width: 100.0%;" title="Background color : Light grey 100%">Numbering</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Use Element Auto-Number</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">false</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">true/false</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Element auto-numbering option to control (turn off or turn on) element numbering functionality for the whole project.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Display Element Number</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">false</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">true/false</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">The 'Display Element Number' option allows to turn on or turn off the representation of the element number.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Lock Element Auto-Numbering</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">false</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">true/false</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">If set to true, the element auto-numbering is locked and cannot be changed.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Check Element Number Uniqueness Including all Properties</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">false</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">true/false</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Set to true to include the main element number together with all internal element numbers into element uniqueness checking.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Check Element Number Uniqueness</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">true</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">true/false</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Set to true to check element number uniqueness.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Check Element Number Uniqueness in:</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">-</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">multi-selection</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Element number uniqueness options allow specifying where your element number should be unique. </td></tr><tr><td class="highlight-#f4f5f7" colspan="4" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7" title="Background colour : Light grey 100%">ReqIF</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Path to the resources</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">&lt;project.dir&gt;</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">custom</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Specify the path to ReqIF resources to allow for exchanging projects among users. The default location is the project directory &lt;project.dir&gt;. Absolut local path, URL, and path variables are supported.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Store ReqIF Metadata Information</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">false</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">true/false</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Set to true to store the ReqIF metadata information in the model.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Relationship Types</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">-</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">custom</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Specify the relationship types to export. Otherwise, all relationship types are exported.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Pre-processing Rules </td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">-</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">custom</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Select rules to apply before export.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Exclude Properties</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">A list of properties</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">-</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Add properties to ignore and not export into the ReqIF file.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Element Type</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">-</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">custom</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Specify an element type to create for the objects you are importing. The default type, unless otherwise specified, is Requirement.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Relation Type</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">-</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">custom</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Specify a relationship type to create for the relationships you are importing. The default type, unless otherwise specified, is DerivedReqt.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Post-processing Rules</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">-</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">custom</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Select rules to apply after import.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Import All Properties</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">true</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">true/false</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Set to true to import all the properties into the model.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Excluded Properties</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">A list of properties</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">-</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Add properties to ignore and not import into the project.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Properties to Ignore for Status Update</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">-</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">custom</td><td style="width: 55.98%;"><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">Specify the properties that, when modified, will not change the elements' status to 'updated'.</span></td></tr><tr><td class="highlight-#f4f5f7" colspan="4" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7" title="Background colour : Light grey 100%">Requirements</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Unredline Patterns in Requirement Text</td><td>false</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">true/false</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Set to true to underline the Requirement text that matches the predefined pattern.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Show Reuse Dialog when Deriving Requirement</td><td>true</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">true/false</td><td style="width: 55.98%;"><p>(This is personal property, meaning its value is unique for each user)</p><p>Show a dialog before reusing the name and text when creating a new Derive relation.</p></td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Reuse Requirement Name and Text</td><td>true</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">true/false</td><td style="width: 55.98%;"><p>(This is personal property, meaning its value is unique for each user)</p><p>Reuse name and text when creating a new Derive relation.</p></td></tr><tr><td class="highlight-#f4f5f7" colspan="4" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7" title="Background colour : Light grey 100%"><p title="">Styling</p></td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Ask to update outdated 'Default' symbol styles</td><td>true</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">true/false</td><td style="width: 55.98%;"><p>Set to true to ask to update outdated 'Default' symbol styles.</p></td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Ask to update outdated 'Defined Elsewhere' symbol styles</td><td>true</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">true/false</td><td style="width: 55.98%;"><p>Set to true to ask to update outdated 'Defined Elsewhere' symbol styles.</p></td></tr><tr><td class="highlight-#f4f5f7" colspan="4" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7" style="width: 100.0%;" title="Background color : Light grey 100%">Suspect Links</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Enable Tracking</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">false</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">true/false</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Set to true to start tracking suspect links.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Track Deprecated Elements</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">true</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">true/false</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Set to true to track element deletions.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Track Links in main Project Only</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">true</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">true/false</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Set to true to track suspect links in the main project only.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Tracking Scope</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">-</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">multi-selection</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Select elements whose suspicions should be tracked.</td></tr><tr><td class="highlight-#f4f5f7" colspan="4" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7" title="Background colour : Light grey 100%">SysML</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Propagate SysML Values</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">true</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">true/false</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">If True, the propagation mechanism is turned on which allows value reconfiguration from the nested structures of the context. Propagated values will be displayed in the initialValue compartment. Set to False to turn off the Value Propagation mechanism. Then the values will be collected only from the specified instances.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Change allocated Behavior context</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">true</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">true/false</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">If enabled, Behavior::context will be the Block to which behavior is allocated.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Rename Property on Type Change</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">false</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">true/false</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">If set to true, the name of the property or port (except Association End) that is default tool-generated is automatically changed to a type name after specifying the type for them. </td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Rename Association End on Type Change</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">true</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">true/false</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">If set to true, the name of the Association End that is default tool-generated is automatically named as a type specified at the role end after connecting or reconnecting the Association. </td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Create Association on Part Creation</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">false</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">true/false</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">If enabled, an association between a part property type and part owner is created automatically.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Include Directed Features into Combined Direction of Proxy Port</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">false</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">true/false</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">If enabled, includes all owned and inherited Directed Features of the property type into derived combined direction of the Proxy Port. Tread Provided as Out, Required as In direction. </td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Show Units</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">false</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">true/false</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">If enabled, the units are shown next to values and can be specified or changed directly on a Value Specification.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Show Type Names in Property Path</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">false</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">true/false</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Use type name in property path &quot;dot notation&quot; when property name is empty.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Allocation Mode</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">Ask and Remember </td><td style="width: 10.3634%;"><p>Definition (Activity allocated to Block)</p><p>Usage (Activity allocated to Part)</p><p>Ask and Remember </p></td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Select the Usage mode to create allocation relationships between an Action and Part Property representing a swimlane in which the action is contained. Select the Definition mode to create an allocation relationship between a behavior of action (Activity) and Block representing a swimlane in which the action is contained.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Item Flow Creation Mode</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">Direct</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;"><p>Between Part Types</p><p>Direct</p></td><td style="width: 55.98%;"><p>Select the mode for managing Item Flow in your project:</p><p>Select &quot;Direct&quot; to create the Item Flow between ports or parts in SysML Internal Block Diagram.</p><p>Select &quot;Between Part Types&quot; to create the Item Flow between Part property types. The &quot;Between Part Types&quot; option also allows you to quickly realize and synchronize Flows on Activity Edges, Connectors, and Messages. </p></td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Remove Not Realized Item Flows</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">false</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">true/false</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Set to true to automatically remove Item Flows after the last element that realizes them is deleted. </td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Creation of Contextual Relationships</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">Disabled</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;"><p>Fully enabled</p><p>Partially Enabled</p><p>Disabled</p></td><td style="width: 55.98%;"><p>Select &quot;Fully Enabled&quot; to establish relationships contextualization throughout the project.</p><p>Select &quot;Partially Enabled&quot; to ignore the contextualization while creating relationships on the diagram pane.</p><p>Select &quot;Disabled&quot; to bypass contextualization.</p></td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Show Item Flows on Affected Diagrams</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">true</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">true/false</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Set to true to update all affected diagrams and show the Conveyed Items of Item Flows realized on Connectors, Activity Edges, Messages, or Associations whenever a new Item Flow is created or an existing one is modified.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Check Item Flow Realization Compatibility</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">true</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">true/false</td><td style="width: 55.98%;"><p>Set to true to check the Item Flow compatibility with the behavior model while running the Behavior to Structure Synchronization. <br />Learn more about <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12PTWRT" ri:content-title="UAF Behavior to structure synchronization" /></ac:link>.</p><p>Learn more about <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="SYSMLPTWRT" ri:content-title="Behavior to structure synchronization" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SysML Behavior to structure synchronization]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p></td></tr><tr><td>Generate Diagram Layout Annotations</td><td>true</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">true/false</td><td style="width: 55.98%;"><p>Set the default option for generating diagram layout annotations for the Modelica export.</p></td></tr><tr><td class="highlight-grey" colspan="4" data-highlight-colour="grey" style="width: 100.0%;" title="Background colour :">UAF</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Architecture Framework</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">-</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">DoDAF, DoDAF 2.0, MODAF, NAF, NAF 4.0, UAF<br />UAF Enterprise Architecture</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">The architecture framework option identifies the environment and a subset of aliases to use within the context of the project (i.e., UAF, DoDAF, MODAF, etc.). To change the framework, go to File &gt; Convert To.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">ISO Date Time format</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">YYYY-MM-DD</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;"><p>Various, according to your locale</p></td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Select ISO Date Time format for setting dates for elements.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Use MIL-STD-2525 Symbology Library</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">None</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">Available library versions</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Specifies which MIL-STD-2525 Symbology Library Version to use for the project.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">BPMN Compliance Mode</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">false</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">true/false</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Specifies whether the compliant BPMN stereotypes should be applied to UAF Elements. The options must be true to create UAF diagrams dependent on the Business Process Diagram.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Map UAF Information Elements on ERD Entities</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">false</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">true/false</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Specifies whether the Entity stereotype should be applied to the UAF Entity Item element. The options must be true to create a UAF Entity Relationship Diagram.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Auto Check Model Completeness</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">false</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">true/false</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">If true, the user will be automatically notified about model incompleteness (recommended for novice UAF users). Note, that the auto-check may decrease the performance of the modeling tool. </td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Respect UAF Information Flows on Generalization</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">true</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">true/false</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Specifies whether the realization of inherited Operational Exchanges/Resource Exchanges is allowed or not. </td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Respect UAF Information Flows on Aggregation</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">true</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">true/false</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Specifies whether the realization of aggregated Operational Exchanges/Resource Exchanges is allowed or not. </td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Default Property Sets owner</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">-</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">-</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Defines the default owner for Property Sets, that are created using Property Set Models such as SV-7, NSV-7, or SvcV-7.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Inform about user read-only projects after conversion</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">true</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">true/false</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Set to true to inform about user read-only projects that haven't been converted during project conversion.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Show default documentation for UAF Packages</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">true</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">true/false</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Specifies whether the default Package documentation should be shown in the UAF Packages Documentation property:<br />Set to true to show the default Package documentation for the newly created UAF Packages.<br />Set to false to create the new UAF Packages without the default documentation. <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12PTWRT" ri:content-title="Show default documentation for UAF packages" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more>>>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;"><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">Sub-resources depth in Strategic Phasing Gantt Chart</span></td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">3</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">1-10</td><td style="width: 55.98%;"><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">Set the level of sub-resources hierarchy displayed in the Capability Provisions panel of the Strategic Phasing, CV-3, StV-3, NCV-3, or Cr Gantt Chart.</span></td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Default Operational Constraints Owner</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">Operational Constraints </td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">-</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Defines the default owner for Operational constraints. Depending on the framework, the default owner is Operational Constraints, Business Rules, OV-6a, NOV-6a, or L8. Change the default owner by choosing a different one from the model.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Default Resource Constraints Owner</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">Resource Constraints </td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">-</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Defines the default owner for Resource constraints. Depending on the framework, the default owner is Resource Constraints, SV-10a, NSV-10a, or P8. Change the default owner by choosing a different one from the model.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Default Service Constraints Owner</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">Service Constraints </td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">-</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Defines the default owner for Service Policies and Service Contracts constraints. Depending on the framework, the default owner for the Service Policies constraints is Service Constraints, SOV-4a, SvcV-10a, or S8 and for the Service Contracts, the default owner is Service Constraints. Change the default owner by choosing a different one from the model.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Default Strategic Constraints Owner</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">Strategic Constraints </td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">-</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Defines the default owner for Strategic constraints. Depending on the framework, the default owner is Strategic Constraints or C8. Change the default owner by choosing a different one from the model.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Default Security Constraints Owner</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;"> Security Constraints </td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">-</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Defines the default owner for Security constraints. Depending on the framework, the default owner is Security Constraints. Change the default owner by choosing a different one from the model.</td></tr><tr><td class="highlight-#f4f5f7" colspan="4" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7" style="width: 100.0%;" title="Background color : Light grey 100%">Validation</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Validation Scope</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">-</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">multi-selection</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Specify the validation scope. The validation scope can be the entire model or some part of it. When the validation is run, each validation rule is evaluated for each suitable element in the validation scope.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Validate only Visible Diagrams</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">true</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">true/false</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Set to true to validate only elements in one or more visible diagrams.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Exclude Elements from Used Read-Only Project</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">true</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">true/false</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Set to true to exclude projects used in read-only mode and the elements that exist in these projects. Excluded projects will not be validated.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Mark in Tree and Diagrams</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">true</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">true/false</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Set to true to highlight the invalid model elements in the browser with a small x symbol. The element symbol will be colored according to the severity level of the problem in the diagram.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Ignored Validation Suites</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">-</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">multi-selection</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Specify the validation suites which should not be run during the model validation.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Ignored Validation Rules</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">-</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">multi-selection</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Specify the validation rules that should not be run during the automatic model validation.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Minimal Severity</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">warning</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;"><p>debug</p><p>info</p><p>warning</p><p>error</p><p>fatal</p></td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Specify the minimal severity. When the validation is running, only the validation rules of the specified and the higher severity level will be run.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Composition Inspection</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">Standard</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;"><p>Standard</p><p>Advanced</p></td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Select the level of composition inspection in your projects. The Standard inspection searches for issues only in the project, while the Advanced inspection, in addition to the Standard inspection, searches for issues in all the used projects. The inspection runs validation rules which validate the correctness of model integrity and project composition.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Detect Illegal Model References in Shared Projects</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">true</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">true/false</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Set to true to check for illegal model references in projects. Illegal references are one of the main reasons why recovered elements appear in shared projects.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Ignored Validation Suites</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">-</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">multi-selection</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Specify the validation suites which should not be run during the passive diagram validation.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Ignored Validation Rules</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">-</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">multi-selection</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Specify the validation rules which should not be run during the passive diagram validation.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Minimal Severity</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">warning</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;"><p>debug</p><p>info</p><p>warning</p><p>error</p><p>fatal</p></td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Specify the minimal severity. When passive diagram validation is running, only the validation rules of the specified and the higher severity level will be run.</td></tr><tr><td class="highlight-#deebff" colspan="4" data-highlight-colour="#deebff" style="width: 100.0%;" title="Background color : Light blue 35%"><strong>Diagram Info</strong></td></tr><tr><td colspan="4" style="width: 100.0%;">Diagram information shows summarized data in each diagram, e.g. the author, creation and modification dates, etc. Choose the diagram tags in standard mode, their values should be shown in a predefined diagram information table, or choose how diagram information should e represented using the HTML code.</td></tr><tr><td class="highlight-#deebff" colspan="4" data-highlight-colour="#deebff" style="width: 100.0%;" title="Background color : Light blue 35%"><strong>Symbol styles</strong></td></tr><tr><td colspan="4" style="width: 100.0%;">Specify the shape, path, diagram, and stereotype symbol properties. Create, edit, clone, import/export, or remove element display styles. : Light grey 100%">Default</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Fill Color</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">RGB [255, 255, 204]</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">Custom</td><td style="width: By default, the appropriate property defined for the diagram is used.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Suspend Auto-Displaying of Label</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">Same as Diagram</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;"><p>Same as Diagram</p><p>true</p><p>false</p></td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Set to true if you do not want to automatically show labels on the diagram pane while editing the model. Also, configure generated code formatting styles for the selected programming languages. <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=258048801 space=VTO version=1 -->
## PAGE 00074: (2026x) Switching between projects with different AF related perspectives

- page_id: `258048801`
- space_key: `VTO`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/VTO/pages/258048801/2026x+Switching+between+projects+with+different+AF+related+perspectives
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-24T15:42:15.418+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Virtual Twin of the Organization Documentation > (2026x) Virtual Twin of the Organization Documentation > (2026x) User Guide > (2026x) Getting started > (2026x) Customizing and Selecting Perspective
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A particular behavior is applied when switching between projects with different Architecture Framework (AF) related perspectives.When you work with two projects using different perspectives than those projects were created with and switch between them, a message is displayed.You can choose whether to change the perspective according to the project's framework or the latest used perspective in that message.

In the following table, see the cases of the actions required when switching between projects A and B.

| Actions of Project A | Project A perspective according to AF | The last used perspective of Project A | Actions of Project B | Project B perspective according to AF | The last used perspective of Project B | Required actions |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Create Project A | UAF | - | Create project B | MODAF | - | The project you are opening/creating requires the MODAF perspective. The perspective will be changed to MODAF automatically. (In the example below, Case #1) |
| Open Project A | UAF | - | Open project B | MODAF | - | The project you are opening/creating requires the MODAF perspective. The perspective will be changed to MODAF automatically. (In the example below, Case #1) |
| Migrate/Open/Create Project A | UAF | - | Migrate project B | MODAF | - | The project you are opening/creating requires the MODAF perspective. The perspective will be changed to MODAF automatically.(In the example below, Case #1) |
| Create Project A | UAF | - | Create project B | UAF | - | No actions |
| Open project A | UAF | - | Open project B | UAF | - | No actions |
| Migrate/Open/Create Project A | UAF | - | Migrate project B | UAF | - | No actions |
| Create Project A | UAF | MODAF | Create project B | MODAF | - | No actions |
| Open Project A | UAF | MODAF | Open project B | MODAF | - | No actions |
| Switch to opened Project A | UAF | MODAF | Switch to opened project B | UAF | - | The project you are opening/creating requires the UAF perspective. The perspective will be changed to UAF automatically.(In the example below, Case #1) |
| Switch to opened Project A | UAF | - | Switch to opened project B | NAF | - | The project you are opening/creating requires the NAF perspective. The perspective will be changed to NAF automatically.(In the example below, Case #1) |
| Switch to opened Project A | UAF | MODAF | Switch to opened project B | NAF | DoDAF 2 | The project you are opening requires the NAF perspective.To continue, choose one of the following:OK to switch the perspective to NAF (according to the framework).Cancel to switch the perspective to DoDAF 2(the latest used perspective).(In the example below, Case #2) |
| Switch to opened Project A | UAF | MODAF | Switch to opened project B | MODAF | DoDAF 2 | The project you are opening requires the MODAF perspective.To continue, choose one of the following:OK to switch the perspective to MODAF (according to the framework).Cancel to switch the perspective to DoDAF 2 (the latest used perspective).(In the example below, Case #2) |

##### Examples of messages

Here are examples of messages you receive when switching projects with different perspectives.

**Case #1.**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

**Case #2.**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

perspective to <strong>MODAF </strong>(according to the framework).</li><li><strong>Cancel</strong><span> </span>to switch the perspective to <strong>DoDAF 2</strong> (the latest used perspective).</li></ul><p>(In the example below, Case #2)</p></td></tr></tbody></table><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><h3 class="auto-cursor-target">Examples of messages</h3><p class="auto-cursor-target">Here are examples of messages you receive when switching projects with different perspectives.</p><p class="auto-cursor-target"><strong>Case #1.</strong></p><p class="auto-cursor-target"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="switching_case_1.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12PTWRT" ri:content-title="Switching between projects with different AF related perspectives" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p class="auto-cursor-target"><strong>Case #2.</strong></p><p class="auto-cursor-target"><ac:image ac:height="201"><ri:attachment ri:filename="switch_projects_diff_perspectives.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12PTWRT" ri:content-title="Switching between projects with different AF related perspectives" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=258048805 space=VTO version=1 -->
## PAGE 00075: (2026x) Toolbars

- page_id: `258048805`
- space_key: `VTO`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/VTO/pages/258048805/2026x+Toolbars
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-24T15:42:15.665+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Virtual Twin of the Organization Documentation > (2026x) Virtual Twin of the Organization Documentation > (2026x) User Guide > (2026x) Getting started > (2026x) Understanding the user interface
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Toolbars help to speed up your work when performing commonly used tasks. Your modeling tool has two types of toolbars:

- Main toolbars - contain commands for basic project-related tasks and are located at the top of the modeling tool window, right under the main menu.
- Diagram toolbars - contain commands for working with diagram symbols and are located at the top of the diagram pane.

To show or hide different toolbars

- In the main menu, click View , select either Main Toolbars or Diagram Toolbars , and in the displayed menu select/clear the check boxes of the toolbars that you want to show/hide.
- Right-click the desired toolbar area and in the shortcut menu select/clear the check boxes of the toolbars that you want to show/hide.

##### Customizing toolbars

You can customize existing toolbars by adding or removing buttons and also create new custom toolbars.

To add a new toolbar

1. Do one of the following:
  - In the main menu, select View > Main Toolbars / Diagram Toolbars > Customize .
  - Right-click the toolbar area, and in the shortcut menu select Customize .
2. In the Customize Toolbars dialog, select the desired toolbar group and click the Add > New Toolbar .
3. Enter the name of the new toolbar and click OK .

After completing the above steps, a new toolbar is created in the selected toolbar group, and you can choose to show/hide it in the shortcut menu of the respective toolbar area. Note that the newly created toolbar does not have any buttons, so you need to add them after creating it.

To add a new button to a toolbar

1. Do one of the following:
  - In the main menu, select View > Main Toolbars / Diagram Toolbars > Customize .
  - Right-click the toolbar area, and in the shortcut menu select Customize .
2. In the Customize Toolbars dialog, select the desired toolbar and click the Add > Button . [ATTACHMENT filename='customize_toolbars_dialog.png']
3. In the Add Button dialog, select the desired button or command and click OK .
4. Click OK one more time to close the Customize Toolbars dialog.

A new button is added to the selected toolbar. To add more than one button, repeat steps 2 and 3 as many times as you need.

To remove a toolbar or button

1. Do one of the following:
  - In the main menu, select View > Main Toolbars / Diagram Toolbars > Customize .
  - Right-click the toolbar area, and in the shortcut menu select Customize .
2. In the Customize Toolbars dialog, select the toolbar or button you want to remove and click the Remove button.

After completing the above steps, the selected toolbar or button is removed from the respective toolbar area or toolbar. Toolbars that have been removed do not appear in the menu for selecting which toolbars you want to show.

#### NOTE: Restoring default toolbars

Restoring default toolbars

To undo all the actions for customizing toolbars, open the **Customize Toolbars** dialog, as described in step 1 above, and click the **Reset to Defaults** button.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>Toolbars help to speed up your work when performing commonly used tasks. Your modeling tool has two types of toolbars:</p><ul><li>Main toolbars - contain commands for basic project-related tasks and are located at the top of the modeling tool window, right under the main menu.</li><li>Diagram toolbars - contain commands for working with diagram symbols and are located at the top of the diagram pane.</li></ul><p><br /></p><p>To show or hide different toolbars</p><hr /><ul><li>In the main menu, click <strong>View</strong>, select either <strong>Main Toolbars</strong> or <strong>Diagram Toolbars</strong>, and in the displayed menu<strong> </strong>select/clear the check boxes of the toolbars that you want to show/hide.</li><li>Right-click the desired toolbar area and in the shortcut menu select/clear the check boxes of the toolbars that you want to show/hide.</li></ul><h3>Customizing toolbars</h3><p>You can customize existing toolbars by adding or removing buttons and also create new custom toolbars.</p><p><br /></p><p>To add a new toolbar</p><hr /><ol><li>Do one of the following:<ul><li>In the main menu, select <strong>View</strong> &gt; <strong>Main Toolbars</strong>/<strong>Diagram Toolbars</strong> &gt; <strong>Customize</strong>.</li><li>Right-click the toolbar area, and in the shortcut menu select <strong>Customize</strong>.</li></ul></li><li>In the <strong>Customize Toolbars</strong> dialog, select the desired toolbar group and click the <strong>Add</strong> &gt;<strong> New Toolbar</strong>.</li><li>Enter the name of the new toolbar and click <strong>OK</strong>. </li></ol><p><br />After completing the above steps, a new toolbar is created in the selected toolbar group, and you can choose to show/hide it in the shortcut menu of the respective toolbar area. Note that the newly created toolbar does not have any buttons, so you need to add them after creating it.</p><p><br /></p><p>To add a new button to a toolbar</p><hr /><ol><li>Do one of the following:<ul><li>In the main menu, select <strong>View</strong> &gt; <strong>Main Toolbars</strong>/<strong>Diagram Toolbars</strong> &gt; <strong>Customize</strong>.</li><li>Right-click the toolbar area, and in the shortcut menu select <strong>Customize</strong>.</li></ul></li><li>In the <strong>Customize Toolbars</strong> dialog, select the desired toolbar and click the <strong>Add</strong> &gt;<strong> Button</strong>.<br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="customize_toolbars_dialog.png" /></ac:image><br /> </li><li>In the <strong>Add Button</strong> dialog, select the desired button or command and click <strong>OK</strong>.</li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong> one more time to close the <strong>Customize Toolbars</strong> dialog.</li></ol><p><br />A new button is added to the selected toolbar. To add more than one button, repeat steps 2 and 3 as many times as you need.</p><p><br /></p><p>To remove a toolbar or button</p><hr /><ol><li>Do one of the following:<ul><li>In the main menu, select <strong>View</strong> &gt; <strong>Main Toolbars</strong>/<strong>Diagram Toolbars</strong> &gt; <strong>Customize</strong>.</li><li>Right-click the toolbar area, and in the shortcut menu select <strong>Customize</strong>.</li></ul></li><li>In the <strong>Customize Toolbars</strong> dialog, select the toolbar or button you want to remove and click the <strong>Remove</strong> button.</li></ol><p><br />After completing the above steps, the selected toolbar or button is removed from the respective toolbar area or toolbar. Toolbars that have been removed do not appear in the menu for selecting which toolbars you want to show.</p><p><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="67428948-3ddd-4460-9978-eac77bf76b73"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Restoring default toolbars</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>To undo all the actions for customizing toolbars, open the <strong>Customize Toolbars</strong> dialog, as described in step 1 above, and click the <strong>Reset to Defaults</strong> button.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=258048775 space=VTO version=1 -->
## PAGE 00076: (2026x) UAF 1.2

- page_id: `258048775`
- space_key: `VTO`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/VTO/pages/258048775/2026x+UAF+1.2
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-24T15:42:14.345+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Virtual Twin of the Organization Documentation > (2026x) Virtual Twin of the Organization Documentation > (2026x) User Guide
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

For the documentation related to UAF 1.2, please see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation' space='UAF12PTWRT'].

The Unified Architecture Framework (UAF) supports the capability to:

- model architectures for a broad range of complex systems, which may include hardware, software, data, personnel, and facility elements;
- model consistent architectures for system-of-systems (SoS) down to lower levels of design and implementation;
- support the analysis, specification, design, and verification of complex systems; and
- improve the ability to exchange architecture information among related tools that are SysML-based and tools that are based on other standards.

According to modeling needs, there are the following UAF templates for different purposes:

- UAF Project for Virtual Twin of the Organization - you can create a project using this template to define a Virtual Twin of your Organization. APQC and ESCO model templates include the structure of processes and people skills relevant to organization modeling.
- UAF Project is designed to model architectures for a broad range of complex systems. The UAF provides an applicable model security controls, threat, risk, and risk mitigation. It allows for defining consistent architectures for System-of-Systems (SoS), including all creation phases from the design until implementation.
- EA with BPMN Project* - u sing this template, you will get a simplified Enterprise Architecture project with BPMN, which is based on the UAF framework and designed for enterprise and IT architecture modeling. Creating an EA with the BPMN project will switch the modeling tool to the EA-BPMN Architect graphical user interface and use the EA with the BPMN model template.
- **UAF Project Template with Process Guide** - using this template, you will get a new UAF project enhanced with a modeling guide. The modeling guide helps you to build architecture by providing summary information about the UAF structure and architecture creation process.
- **EA Project Template with Process Guide** - using this template, you will get a new UAF project enhanced with the Enterprise Architecture Guide for UAF (a non-normative component of the UAF specification). This modeling guide aids in building architecture by providing nine steps of workflow.
- **Blank UAF Project** - creates a blank UAF project.

Please make sure, that the UAF 1.2 Plugin is [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Installing plugins' space='ILTWRT'].

*Starting with the 2024x version, the **UAF Enterprise Architecture Project**was renamed to**EA with BPMN Project.**

****

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### UAF 1.2 Grid. The grid is a way of showing how the various view specifications (cells) correspond to viewpoints (prev. known as domains) (horizontal rows) and the aspects (prev. known as model kinds) (the columns) that describe the view specification.

The descriptions of all the viewpoints are provided in the following table:

| Viewpoint | Acronym | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Architecture Management | Am | Identifies the metadata required to develop a suitable architecture that is fit for its purpose. |
| Strategic | St | Capability management process. Describes the capability taxonomy, composition, dependencies, and evolution. |
| Operational | Op | Illustrates the Logical Architecture of the enterprise. Describes the requirements, operational behavior, structure, and exchanges required to support (exhibit) capabilities. Defines all operational elements in an implementation/solution-independent manner. |
| Services | Sv | The Service-Orientated View (SOV) is a description of services needed to directly support the operational domain as described in the Operational View. A service withinMODAF is understood in its broadest sense, as a unit of work through which a provider provides a useful result to a consumer.DoDAF: The Service Views within the Services Viewpoint describe the design for service-based solutions to support operational development processes (JCIDS) and Defense Acquisition System or capability development within the Joint Capability Areas. |
| Personnel | Ps | Defines and explores organizational resource types. Shows the taxonomy of types of organizational resources as well as connections, interaction, and growth over time. |
| Resources | Rs | Captures a solution architecture consisting of resources, e.g., organizational, software, artifacts, capability configurations, and natural resources that implement the operational requirements. Further design of a resource is typically detailed in SysML or UML. |
| Security | Sc | Security assets and security enclaves. Defines the hierarchy of security assets and asset owners, security constraints (policy, laws, and guidance), and details where they are located (security enclaves). |
| Projects | Pj | Describes projects and project milestones, how those projects deliver capabilities, the organizations contributing to the projects, and dependencies between projects. |
| Standards | Sd | MODAF: Technical Standards Views are extended from the core DoDAF views to include non-technical standards such as operational doctrine, industry process standards, etc.DoDAF: The Standards Views within the Standards Viewpoint are the set of rules governing the arrangement, interaction, and interdependence of solution parts or elements. |
| Actual Resources | Ar | The analysis, e.g., evaluation of different alternatives, what-if, trade-offs, V&V on the actual resource configurations. Illustrates the expected or achieved actual resource configurations. |

The descriptions of all the aspects are provided in the following table:

| Aspect | Acronym | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Motivation | Mv | Captures motivational elements e.g., challenges, opportunities, and concerns, that pertain to enterprise transformation efforts, and different types of requirements, e.g., operational, services, personnel, resources, or security controls. |
| Taxonomy | Tx | Presents all the elements as a standalone structure. Presents all the elements as a specialization hierarchy, provides a text definition for each one and references the source of the element. |
| Structure | Sr | Describes the breakdown of structural elements e.g. logical performers, systems, projects, etc. into their smaller parts. |
| Connectivity | Cn | Describes the connections, relationships, and interactions between the different elements. |
| Processes | Pr | Captures activity-based behavior and flows. It describes activities, their Inputs/Outputs, activity actions, and flows between them. |
| States | St | Captures state-based behavior of an element. It is a graphical representation of states of a structural element and how it responds to various events and actions. |
| Sequences | Sq | Expresses a time-ordered examination of the exchanges as a result of a particular scenario. Provides a time-ordered examination of the exchanges between participating elements as a result of a particular scenario. |
| Information | If | Address the information perspective on operational, service, and resource architectures. Allows analysis of an architecture’s information and data definition aspect, without consideration of implementation-specific issues. |
| Parameters | Pm | Shows the measurable properties of something in the physical world and elements and relationships that are involved in defining the environments applicable to capability, operational concept or set of systems. |
| Constraints | Ct | Details the measurements that set performance requirements constraining capabilities. Also defines the rules governing behavior and structure. |
| Roadmap | Rm | Addresses how elements in the architecture change over time. |
| Traceability | Tr | Describes the mapping between elements in the architecture. This can be between different viewpoints within domains as well as between domains. It can also be between structure and behaviors. |

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

APQC and ESCO model templates include the structure of processes and people skills relevant to organization modeling.</li><li><strong>UAF Project</strong> is designed to model architectures for a broad range of complex systems. The modeling guide helps you to build architecture by providing summary information about the UAF structure and architecture creation process.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);"><strong>EA Project Template with Process Guide</strong> - using this template, you will get a new UAF project enhanced with the Enterprise Architecture Guide for UAF (a non-normative component of the UAF specification). This modeling guide aids in building architecture by providing nine steps of workflow.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);"><strong>Blank UAF Project</strong> - creates a blank UAF project.</span></li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="36302a13-0993-4e01-9260-10ef8aff2505"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Please make sure, that the UAF 1.2 Plugin is <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="ILTWRT" ri:content-title="Installing plugins" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[installed]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><hr /><p>*Starting with the 2024x version, the <strong style="text-align: left;">UAF Enterprise Architecture Project</strong><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);"><span> </span>was renamed to<span> </span></span><strong style="text-align: left;">EA with BPMN Project.</strong></p><p><strong style="text-align: left;">      </strong></p><p><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:width="1319"><ri:attachment ri:filename="UAF _1_2 grid Last.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) UAF 1.2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">UAF 1.2 Grid. The grid is a way of showing how the various view specifications (cells) correspond to viewpoints (prev. known as domains) (horizontal rows) and the aspects (prev. known as model kinds) (the columns) that describe the view specification. </h6><p>The descriptions of all the viewpoints are provided in the following table:</p><table class="relative-table wrapped" style="width: 93.8014%;"><colgroup class=""> <col class="" style="width: 7.89936%;" /> <col class="" style="width: 4.73961%;" /> <col class="" style="width: 87.361%;" /> </colgroup><tbody class=""><tr class=""><th><p align="center"><strong>Viewpoint</strong></p></th><th><p align="center"><strong>Acronym</strong></p></th><th><p align="center"><strong>Description</strong></p></th></tr><tr class=""><th><p>Architecture Management</p></th><td style="text-align: center;"><p>Am</p></td><td><p>Identifies the metadata required to develop a suitable architecture that is fit for its purpose.</p></td></tr><tr class=""><th><p>Strategic</p></th><td style="text-align: center;"><p>St</p></td><td><p>Capability management process. ## PAGE 00077: (2026x) Understanding the user interface

- page_id: `258048667`
- space_key: `VTO`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/VTO/pages/258048667/2026x+Understanding+the+user+interface
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-24T15:42:05.623+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Virtual Twin of the Organization Documentation > (2026x) Virtual Twin of the Organization Documentation > (2026x) User Guide > (2026x) Getting started
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The user interface is where you will control your software installation. The modeling tool window contains the following:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Main menu' space='MDTWRT']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Toolbars' space='MDTWRT']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Model Browser' space='MDTWRT']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Toolbars' space='MDTWRT']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Customizing diagram palette' space='MDTWRT']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Status line' space='MDTWRT']

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

Watch this demo to get familiar with the modeling tool working environment, main concepts, and functions.

800700

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The user interface is where you will control your software installation. The modeling tool window contains the following:</p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Main menu" ri:space-key="MDTWRT" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Toolbars" ri:space-key="MDTWRT" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Main toolbar]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Model Browser" ri:space-key="MDTWRT" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Toolbars" ri:space-key="MDTWRT" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Diagram toolbar]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Customizing diagram palette" ri:space-key="MDTWRT" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Diagram palette]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Status line" ri:space-key="MDTWRT" /></ac:link></li></ul><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="modeling_tool_user_interface.png" /></ac:image></p><p><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="cfa42dd3-36a7-4bb4-a8bc-541d308190a2"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Watch this demo to get familiar with the modeling tool working environment, main concepts, and functions.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><ac:structured-macro ac:name="widget" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="43ebacc9-cb6f-487b-9e93-a2fa63fe2c9c"><ac:parameter ac:name="width">800</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="url"><ri:url ri:value="https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=0B76lH-QtrM&amp;" /></ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="height">700</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></span></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=258048760 space=VTO version=1 -->
## PAGE 00079: (2026x) Using APQC classification

- page_id: `258048760`
- space_key: `VTO`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/VTO/pages/258048760/2026x+Using+APQC+classification
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-24T15:42:10.356+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Virtual Twin of the Organization Documentation > (2026x) Virtual Twin of the Organization Documentation > (2026x) User Guide > (2026x) Procedures
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**APQC (American Productivity & Quality Center)** is a leading authority on benchmarking, best practices, process and performance improvement, and knowledge management (KM).

**APQC’s Process Classification Framework® (PCF)** is a taxonomy of business processes that allows organizations to objectively track and compare their performance internally and externally with organizations from any industry.

The VTO plug-in includes data from **PCF version 7.4.0** that is ready to use for the Enterprise Architect. This data is organized using two notations:

- **APQC-PCF classification:** top level structure organized in packages****

**L1. Category:**the highest level of the process framework. Thirteen Categories have been defined, such as “Develop and Manage Products and Services” or “Develop and Manage Human Capital."

**L2. Process Group:**indicates a group of processes that are part of executing a Category.

- **Ready-to-use APQC-PCF templates:** a low-level structure modeled with Standard Operational Activities.

**L3. Process:** a single process; it is the name for a series of steps required to achieve a particular result

**L4. Activity:** a key step performed to execute a process.

**L5. Task**: an element of work that goes into executing an activity. Tasks are the most fine-grained elements of the PCF, and they often vary among industries and organizations.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><strong>APQC (American Productivity &amp; Quality Center)</strong> is a leading authority on benchmarking, best practices, process and performance improvement, and knowledge management (KM).</p><p><strong>APQC’s Process Classification Framework® (PCF)</strong> is a taxonomy of business processes that allows organizations to objectively track and compare their performance internally and externally with organizations from any industry. </p><p>The VTO plug-in includes data from <strong>PCF version 7.4.0</strong> that is ready to use for the Enterprise Architect. This data is organized using two notations:</p><ul><li><p><strong>APQC-PCF classification:</strong> top level structure organized in packages<strong><br /></strong></p></li></ul><p style="margin-left: 40.0px;"><strong>L1. Category:<span> </span></strong>the highest level of the process framework. Thirteen Categories have been defined, such as “Develop and Manage Products and Services” or “Develop and Manage Human Capital.&quot;</p><p style="margin-left: 40.0px;"><strong>L2. Process Group:<span> </span></strong>indicates a group of processes that are part of executing a Category.</p><ul><li><p><strong>Ready-to-use APQC-PCF templates:</strong> a low-level structure modeled with Standard Operational Activities.</p></li></ul><p style="margin-left: 40.0px;"><strong>L3. Process<span>:</span></strong> a single process; it is the name for a series of steps required to achieve a particular result</p><p style="margin-left: 40.0px;"><strong>L4. Activity<span>:</span></strong> a key step performed to execute a process.</p><p style="margin-left: 40.0px;"><strong>L5. Task</strong>: an element of work that goes into executing an activity. Tasks are the most fine-grained elements of the PCF, and they often vary among industries and organizations.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="APQC data.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Using APQC classification" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=258048782 space=VTO version=1 -->
## PAGE 00080: (2026x) Using ESCO classification

- page_id: `258048782`
- space_key: `VTO`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/VTO/pages/258048782/2026x+Using+ESCO+classification
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-24T15:42:14.617+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Virtual Twin of the Organization Documentation > (2026x) Virtual Twin of the Organization Documentation > (2026x) User Guide > (2026x) Procedures
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**ESCO (European Skills, Competences, Qualifications and Occupations)** is the European multilingual classification of Skills, Competencies, and Occupations.

ESCO works as a **dictionary,** describing, identifying, and classifying professional occupations and skills relevant to the labor market, along with education and training.

Using ESCO provides multiple values:

- Improved Job Matching & Skills Development: Standardized terminology for skills and business roles enhancing job matching, reducing skills gaps, and supporting personalized learning paths.
- Cross-Border and Cross-Value Chain Consistency & Mobility: Multilingual standardization facilitates the recognition of skills and qualifications across countries, boosting workforce mobility, and standardizing HR processes.
- Data-Driven Insights & Policy Alignment: Roles and skills standardization enables access to labor market data for organizational process benchmarking.

The VTO plug-in includes data from **ESCO version 1.2.0** that is ready to use for the Enterprise Architect. The data is organized into two categories: Occupations (aka roles), and Competencies (aka skills). Relations have been imported between roles and skills, but only essential skills have been considered.

UAF types from the Personnel viewpoint are used for representing ESCO data: **UAF Post**for modeling ESCO Occupations, and **UAF Competence** for modeling ESCO Competencies. 
Relations use the **UAF Requires Competence** relation.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><strong>ESCO (European Skills, Competences, Qualifications and Occupations)</strong> is the European multilingual classification of Skills, Competencies, and Occupations.  </p><p>ESCO works as a <strong>dictionary,</strong> describing, identifying, and classifying professional occupations and skills relevant to the labor market, along with education and training. </p><p>Using ESCO provides multiple values:</p><ul><li><strong>Improved Job Matching &amp; Skills Development:</strong><span> </span>Standardized terminology for skills and business roles enhancing job matching, reducing skills gaps, and supporting personalized learning paths.</li><li><strong>Cross-Border and Cross-Value Chain Consistency &amp; Mobility:</strong><span> </span>Multilingual standardization facilitates the recognition of skills and qualifications across countries, boosting workforce mobility, and standardizing HR processes.</li><li><strong>Data-Driven Insights &amp; Policy Alignment:</strong><span> </span>Roles and skills standardization enables access to labor market data for organizational process benchmarking.</li></ul><p>The VTO plug-in includes data from <strong>ESCO version 1.2.0</strong> that is ready to use for the Enterprise Architect. The data is organized into two categories: Occupations (aka roles), and Competencies (aka skills). Relations have been imported between roles and skills, but only essential skills have been considered.</p><p>UAF types from the Personnel viewpoint are used for representing ESCO data: <strong>UAF Post </strong>for modeling ESCO Occupations, and <strong>UAF Competence</strong> for modeling ESCO Competencies.<br />Relations use the <strong>UAF Requires Competence</strong> relation.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="ESCO data.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="(2026x) Using ESCO classification" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=258048688 space=VTO version=1 -->
## PAGE 00081: (2026x) Using the Model Browser

- page_id: `258048688`
- space_key: `VTO`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/VTO/pages/258048688/2026x+Using+the+Model+Browser
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-24T15:42:07.627+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Virtual Twin of the Organization Documentation > (2026x) Virtual Twin of the Organization Documentation > (2026x) User Guide > (2026x) Getting started > (2026x) Understanding the user interface > (2026x) Model Browser
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Model Browser is displayed when at least one project is open. By default, the Model Browser is placed on the left side of the main window. It is possible to move the Model Browser to any place on the window. Also, all tabs can be viewed separately and you can set up the Model Browser according to your needs by hiding the desired tabs. 
 
To change the size of either part of the Model Browser

- Drag the bar that separates the two parts.

To zoom the Model Browser

1. Select a tab in the Model Browser.
2. Use one of the following shortcuts:

- 
  - For Mac

1. 
  1. 
    1. Cmd+Mouse scroll wheel
    2. Cmd+Numpad +/-

- 
  - For Windows

1. 
  1. 
    1. Ctrl+Mouse scroll wheel
    2. Ctrl+Numpad +/-

To change the Model Browser position

- Using the **Options** menu

1. 
  1. From the **Options** menu, select **Environment**. The **Environment Options** dialog opens.
  2. Click on the **Browser** option group.
  3. In the Browser environment options pane, change the **Browser Position** property to **Right** or **Left**.

- Using Toggle 
In the upper-right corner of the selected tab in the Model Browser, click the Toggle auto-hide [IMAGE alt='' src=''] or Toggle floating [IMAGE alt='' src=''] button. This action changes the layout of the interface.

To close or reopen the desired tab of the Model Browser

- Using the **Window** menu, choose the Model Browser tab you want to close or open.

To reset all Model Browser tabs to the default position

- From the **Window** menu, choose the **Reset Windows Configuration** command.

To sort items in the Model Browser alphabetically

1. From the Options menu, select Environment. The Environment Options dialog opens.
2. Click the Browser option group.
3. In the Browser options pane, set the Sort Alphabetically value to true (the default value is true).
