<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=258048661 space=VTO version=1 -->
## PAGE 00082: (2026x) Virtual Twin of the Organization Documentation

- page_id: `258048661`
- space_key: `VTO`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/VTO/pages/258048661/2026x+Virtual+Twin+of+the+Organization+Documentation
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-24T15:42:05.546+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Virtual Twin of the Organization Documentation
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Govern the strategic business transformation from modeling and simulation leveraging the virtual twin of enterprise transformation.**

Using the**Virtual Twin of the Organization (VTO) plug-in**, the**Enterprise Architects**will benefit from the formal**modelling**of the enterprise and necessary resources, leveraging:

- UAF (Unified Architecture Framework) to provide a holistic view of complex organizations, ensuring alignment across multiple domains while offering traceability, impact analysis, and flexibility.
- APQC for consistent process modeling across departments, functions and regions, to identify inefficiencies, enhance performance, and align processes with business objectives and strategies
- ESCO to align workforce skills and job roles with organization needs

From the enterprise model created in CATIA Magic, using the**Collaborative Designer for CATIA Magic role**, data can be pushed to the**3DEXPERIENCE Platform**.

**VTO Data Perspectives**will leverage Virtual Twin data and allow **n****avigating the enterprise operational model** for a better understanding of how people in business fit into this framework. It will also enhance collaboration with colleagues, by revealing the contribution of each role to enterprise processes and deliverables globally or in the context of a project.

For more information about working with VTO, see:

1

````html

It will also enhance collaboration with colleagues, by revealing the contribution of each role to enterprise processes and deliverables globally or in the context of a project.</span></p><p><br /></p><p>For more information about working with VTO, see:</p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="3ada6501-6955-4f62-a409-1ab429a46a17"><ac:parameter ac:name="depth">1</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p><p><br /></p><p><br /></p><p><br /></p><p>       </p>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=258048785 space=VTO version=1 -->
## PAGE 00083: (2026x) Working with model elements in the Model Browser

- page_id: `258048785`
- space_key: `VTO`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/VTO/pages/258048785/2026x+Working+with+model+elements+in+the+Model+Browser
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-24T15:42:14.712+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Virtual Twin of the Organization Documentation > (2026x) Virtual Twin of the Organization Documentation > (2026x) User Guide > (2026x) Getting started > (2026x) Understanding the user interface > (2026x) Model Browser
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

In the Model Browser, you can do the various actions with model elements, such as rename, cut, copy, delete, and others. These actions are available from the particular model element shortcut menu. See the list of concrete actions in the following table.

| Command | Function |
| --- | --- |
| Rename | Switches the element name into the edit mode. You can also press F2 for this. |
| Copy | Copies the selected element. |
| Copy URL | Copies a model element URL to a clipboard. |
| Copy Element Hyperlink | Copies the hyperlink of the selected element. |
| Cut | Cuts the selected element. |
| Paste | Pastes the copied element. |
| Delete | Deletes the selected element. |

To copy/cut and paste the selected model element between different projects

1. Select the model element in the Model Browser. 
 2. From the model element shortcut menu, select **Copy** or **Cut**. 
 3. Open the project to which you want to paste the selected element(s). 
 4. Select the container, where you wish to paste the element(s). 
 5. From the container's shortcut menu, select **Paste**.

To change the location of an element in the Model Browser

1. Select the element in the Model Browser. 
 2. Drag it to the new location (possible owner).

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

These actions are available from the particular model element shortcut menu. Select the model element in the Model Browser.<br /> 2. From the model element shortcut menu, select <strong>Copy</strong> or <strong>Cut</strong>.<br /> 3. Open the project to which you want to paste the selected element(s).<br /> 4. Select the container, where you wish to paste the element(s).<br /> 5. Select the element in the Model Browser.<br /> 2. Drag it to the new location (possible owner).</p>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=258048750 space=VTO version=1 -->
## PAGE 00084: (2026x) Zoom tab

- page_id: `258048750`
- space_key: `VTO`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/VTO/pages/258048750/2026x+Zoom+tab
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-09-24T15:42:10.002+02:00`
- ancestors: Versions of Virtual Twin of the Organization Documentation > (2026x) Virtual Twin of the Organization Documentation > (2026x) User Guide > (2026x) Getting started > (2026x) Understanding the user interface > (2026x) Model Browser
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The **Zoom** tab enables you to preview a selected diagram. To select a diagram, go to the **Containment** or **Diagram** tab. You can also use the **Zoom** tab to zoom in or out of the active diagram.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The Zoom tab in the quick properties panel.

To open the **Zoom** tab, do one of the following

- In the Model Browser, click the Zoom tab.
- On the Window menu, click Zoom .

To zoom in or out of the active diagram

- In the Zoom tab, drag the corners of the blue square.

To quickly access any part of the diagram

- In the Zoom tab, drag the blue square to the desired part of the diagram.

The selected diagram part will be displayed on the diagram pane.

To fit the diagram to the window

- In the Zoom tab, on the blue square [ATTACHMENT filename='fit_window_button.png'] , click the Fit in Window button or press CTRL+W .

To show the **Diagram Zoom Slider**

1. From the Options menu, select Environment . The Environment Options window opens.
2. Select the General options group > Display .
3. In the Display category, select the Show Diagram Zoom Slider check box.

The **Diagram Zoom Slider** is displayed in the **Zoom** tab (see the figure above).

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The <strong>Zoom</strong> tab enables you to preview a selected diagram. To select a diagram, go to the <strong>Containment</strong> or <strong>Diagram</strong> tab. You can also use the <strong>Zoom</strong> tab to zoom in or out of the active diagram.</p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:title="Zoom tab" ac:alt="Zoom tab"><ri:attachment ri:filename="zoom_tab.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="margin-left: 30.0px;text-align: center;">The Zoom tab in the quick properties panel.</h6><p>To open the <strong>Zoom</strong> tab, do one of the following</p><hr /><ul><li>In the Model Browser, click the <strong>Zoom</strong> tab.</li><li>On the <strong>Window</strong> menu, click <strong>Zoom</strong>.</li></ul><p><br class="atl-forced-newline" /> To zoom in or out of the active diagram</p><hr /><ul><li>In the Zoom tab, drag the corners of the blue square.</li></ul><p><br class="atl-forced-newline" /> To quickly access any part of the diagram</p><hr /><ul><li>In the <strong>Zoom</strong> tab, drag the blue square to the desired part of the diagram.</li></ul><p>The selected diagram part will be displayed on the diagram pane.</p><p><br class="atl-forced-newline" /> To fit the diagram to the window</p><hr /><ul><li>In the <strong>Zoom</strong> tab, on the blue square <ac:image ac:height="28" ac:width="26"><ri:attachment ri:filename="fit_window_button.png" /></ac:image>, click the <strong>Fit in Window</strong> button or press <strong>CTRL+W</strong>.</li></ul><p><br class="atl-forced-newline" /> To show the <strong>Diagram Zoom Slider</strong></p><hr /><ol><li>From the <strong>Options</strong> menu, select <strong>Environment</strong>. The <strong>Environment Options</strong> window opens.</li><li>Select the <strong>General</strong> options group &gt; <strong>Display</strong>.</li><li>In the <strong>Display</strong> category, select the <strong>Show Diagram Zoom Slider</strong> check box.</li></ol><p>The <strong>Diagram Zoom Slider</strong> is displayed in the <strong>Zoom</strong> tab (see the figure above).</p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249578298 space=VTO version=1 -->
## PAGE 00090: Creating a project

- page_id: `249578298`
- space_key: `VTO`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/VTO/pages/249578298/Creating+a+project
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-04-29T15:49:58.310+02:00`
- ancestors: Virtual Twin of the Organization Documentation > User Guide > Getting started
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A project is a physical working unit that consists of model, model visualizations(diagrams, tables, matrices, etc.), configuration data and referencesto other elements residing in used projects.

To create a project

1. Do one of the following:
  - Select **File**> **New** **Project**.
  - On the main toolbar, click the **New** **Project** button.
  - Press Ctrl+Shift+N.
2. In the **New****Project** dialog, under **Enterprise Modeling**, select the****project type you want to create.
3. Specify name, location, and other preferences if needed.
4. Click **OK** when you are done.

By default, **UAF Project for Virtual Twin of the Organization** will be selected.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249578304 space=VTO version=1 -->
## PAGE 00092: Customize Perspectives dialog

- page_id: `249578304`
- space_key: `VTO`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/VTO/pages/249578304/Customize+Perspectives+dialog
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-04-29T17:09:12.287+02:00`
- ancestors: Virtual Twin of the Organization Documentation > User Guide > Getting started > Customizing and Selecting Perspective
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To open the Customize Perspectives dialog

- From the **Options**menu, choose **Perspectives**and then **Customize**.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

| Element Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Clone SelectedPerspective | Copy the selected perspective to a new one. |
| Rename SelectedPerspective | The Enter Perspective Name dialog opens. Change the name of the perspective and click OK. You can also rename perspectives by pressing F2. |
| Remove SelectedPerspective | Delete the selected perspective from the list. |
| Import NewPerspective | The Open dialog appears. Select *.umd extension file and click Open to import the perspective into the modeling tool environment. |
| Export SelectedPerspective | The Save dialog opens. Type a name for the created perspective, and click Save to store it as *.umd extension file. |
| Perspective names | List of the available perspectives with the predefined configuration. |
| Customizable elements | List of customizable toolbars and command sections. |
| Edit | Click the Edit button to open the Customize Main Menu dialog.Change the command mode by selecting the radio buttons. |
| Description | Learn the short description of each selected area. |
| Reset to Defaults | Reset all changes back to the default configuration. |

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249578300 space=VTO version=1 -->
## PAGE 00093: Customizing and Selecting Perspective

- page_id: `249578300`
- space_key: `VTO`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/VTO/pages/249578300/Customizing+and+Selecting+Perspective
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-01-02T14:51:46.277+01:00`
- ancestors: Virtual Twin of the Organization Documentation > User Guide > Getting started
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

278424292

278424294

278424293

The Startup dialog opens when you launch a modeling tool (Magic Software Architect, Magic Cyber Systems Engineer, Magic Systems of Systems Architect, MagicDraw, Cameo Systems Modeler, or Cameo Enterprise Architecture) for the first time. Select your work perspective in this dialog.

Due to their growing numbers, many features may be configured for the standard or expert user. The tool better satisfies the needs of different software development process roles by using Perspective.

Use perspectives for:

- Selecting a predefined configuration and features according to your software development process role.
- Finding features faster, because there are fewer.
- Choosing a suitable experience mode with a single click.
- Customizing a set of predefined features and configurations based on user needs.

There are 15 perspectives you can choose from. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Select Perspective dialog' space='VTO']>]]>

To set the perspective for the modeling tool environment

- Launch the program for the first time. The **Startup**dialog opens where you canswitch between perspectives. Select the desired perspective from the list and click **OK**.
- From the **Options**menu, choose **Perspectives**> **Perspectives**. The **Select****Perspective**dialog opens. Select the desired perspective and click the **Apply**button.
- On the **Perspectives**toolbar, select the desired perspective from the list.

278424290

**Related pages**

true

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249578327 space=VTO version=1 -->
## PAGE 00094: Customizing environment options

- page_id: `249578327`
- space_key: `VTO`

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249578335 space=VTO version=1 -->
## PAGE 00111: Process Guide for VTO

- page_id: `249578335`
- space_key: `VTO`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/VTO/pages/249578335/Process+Guide+for+VTO
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-05-12T18:31:49.003+02:00`
- ancestors: Virtual Twin of the Organization Documentation > User Guide > Procedures
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

When selecting a **UAF Project for Virtual Twin of the Organization** template upon creating a project, the new project will include a Process guide.

The Process guide describes the different steps that must be performed to create a Virtual Twin of an Organization (VTO). It provides details on the data to be created by the Enterprise Architect, which enables populating the **VTO Data Perspectives** that will be made available on the 3DEXPERIENCE Platform.

The Process guide focuses on **3 UAF viewpoints** in which data should be created: **Personnel**, **Resources**and **Projects.**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>When selecting a <strong>UAF Project for Virtual Twin of the Organization</strong> template upon creating a project, the new project will include a Process guide. </p><p>The Process guide describes the different steps that must be performed to create a Virtual Twin of an Organization (VTO). It provides details on the data to be created by the Enterprise Architect, which enables populating the <strong>VTO Data Perspectives</strong> that will be made available on the 3DEXPERIENCE Platform.</p><p>The Process guide focuses on <strong>3 UAF viewpoints</strong> in which data should be created: <strong>Personnel</strong>, <strong>Resources </strong>and <strong>Projects.</strong></p><p><br /></p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="VTO_process_guide.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="VTO" ri:content-title="Process Guide for VTO" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249578302 space=VTO version=1 -->
## PAGE 00115: Select Perspective dialog

- page_id: `249578302`
- space_key: `VTO`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/VTO/pages/249578302/Select+Perspective+dialog
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-04-29T16:57:01.259+02:00`
- ancestors: Virtual Twin of the Organization Documentation > User Guide > Getting started > Customizing and Selecting Perspective
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

When you launch a modeling tool for the first time, the **Select Perspective**dialog appears with the ability to switch between perspectives.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

| Element Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Business Analyst | This perspective provides features for Business Analysts. The role is responsible for defining business architecture. Code engineering, transformations, extensions, and other non-related features are hidden. |
| Business Architect | This perspective provides features for Business Architects. The role is responsible for defining business architecture. Code engineering, transformations, extensions, and other non-related features are hidden. |
| DoDAF 2.0 Architect | This perspective provides features dedicated to DoDAF 2.0 Architects. UML modeling features, code engineering, transformations, and other non-DoDAF 2.0-related features are hidden. |
| DODAF Architect | This perspective provides features dedicated to DoDAF Architects. UML modeling features, code engineering, transformations, and other non-DoDAF-related features are hidden. |
| MODAF Architect | This perspective provides features dedicated to MODAF Architects. UML modeling features, code engineering, transformations, and other non-MODAF-related features are hidden. |
| NAF 4.0 Architect | This perspective provides features dedicated to NAF 4.0 Architects. UML modeling features, code engineering, transformations, and other non-NAF 4.0-related features are hidden. |
| NAF Architect | This perspective provides features dedicated to NAF Architects. UML modeling features, code engineering, transformations, and other non-NAF-related features are hidden. |
| SOA Engineer | This perspective is a set of roles primarily involved in designing and implementing projects: Software Architect, Designer, Interface Designer, and Database Designer. This is the default Magic Systems of Systems Architect and Cameo Enterprise Architect configuration. All functionalities are available in expert mode. |
| Software Architect | This perspective is dedicated to Software Architect, Designer, Interface Designer, and Database Designer roles that design and implement the project. All functionalities are available in expert mode. |
| System Analyst | This perspective is a set of roles primarily dedicated to eliciting requirements and modeling the system. Analysis features are highlighted. Code engineering, transformations, and other non-related features are hidden. |
| System Engineer | This perspective provides features dedicated to System Engineers. SysML support is accessible. Requirements elicitation and modeling features are highlighted. UML modeling features, code engineering, transformations, and other non-system-modeling-related features are hidden. |
| TOGAF Architect | This perspective provides features for TOGAF Architects. The role is responsible for defining business architecture. Code engineering, transformations, extensions, and other non-related features are hidden. |
| UAF Architect VTO | The perspective provides features dedicated to UAF Architects for creation of a Virtual Twin of the Organization. This perspective reduces the standard set of menus/commands show in non-expert mode. UML modeling features, code engineering, transformations, and other non-VTO-related features are hidden. |
| UAF Architect | The perspective provides features dedicated to UAF Architects with all the UAF products available. UML modeling features, code engineering, transformations, and other non-UAF-related features are hidden. |
| EA-BPMN Architect* | This perspective provides features dedicated to Enterprise and IT Architects with the essential elements for this area: capabilities, requirements, operational behaviors (with BPMN), resources (hardware, software, facilities), data, and personnel. The user interface is simplified; advanced UAF elements and diagrams are hidden. |
| Zachman Framework | This Quick Start perspective provides basic features dedicated to modeling and not an overpopulated interface for quick learning. Code engineering, transformations, and other advanced features are hidden but easily accessible in the expert mode of this perspective. |
| Customize | Open the Customize Perspectives dialog to customize the selected perspective. You can make your tool interface less complicated by hiding irrelevant features or commands. For example, you can group functional features, and show, or hide selected commands. |

*Starting with the 2024x version, the **UAF Enterprise Architecture Project**was renamed to**EA with BPMN Project.**

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">When you launch a modeling tool for the first time, the <span><strong>Select Perspective </strong><span>dialog appears with the ability to switch <span>between perspectives.</span></span></span></span></p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"><span> <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="VTO_perspective.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="VTO" ri:content-title="Select Perspective dialog" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></span></span></p><table class="wrapped relative-table" style="width: 76.0731%;"><colgroup><col style="width: 15.6799%;" /><col style="width: 84.28%;" /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th>Element Name</th><th>Description</th></tr><tr><th>Business Analyst</th><td>This perspective provides features for Business Analysts. UML modeling features, code engineering, transformations, and other non-NAF-related features are hidden.</td></tr><tr><th>SOA Engineer</th><td>This perspective is a set of roles primarily involved in designing and implementing projects: Software Architect, Designer, Interface Designer, and Database Designer. Code engineering, transformations, and other non-related features are hidden.</td></tr><tr><th>System Engineer</th><td>This perspective provides features dedicated to System Engineers. UML modeling features, code engineering, transformations, and other non-UAF-related features are hidden. </td></tr><tr><th>EA-BPMN Architect*</th><td>This perspective provides features dedicated to Enterprise and IT Architects with the essential elements for this area: capabilities, requirements, operational behaviors (with BPMN), resources (hardware, software, facilities), data, and personnel. The user interface is simplified; advanced UAF elements and diagrams are hidden. </td></tr><tr><th>Zachman Framework</th><td>This Quick Start perspective provides basic features dedicated to modeling and not an overpopulated interface for quick learning. <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249578307 space=VTO version=1 -->
## PAGE 00117: Switching between projects with different AF related perspectives

- page_id: `249578307`
- space_key: `VTO`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/VTO/pages/249578307/Switching+between+projects+with+different+AF+related+perspectives
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-05-11T13:15:59.842+02:00`
- ancestors: Virtual Twin of the Organization Documentation > User Guide > Getting started > Customizing and Selecting Perspective
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A particular behavior is applied when switching between projects with different Architecture Framework (AF) related perspectives.When you work with two projects using different perspectives than those projects were created with and switch between them, a message is displayed.You can choose whether to change the perspective according to the project's framework or the latest used perspective in that message.

In the following table, see the cases of the actions required when switching between projects A and B.

| Actions of Project A | Project A perspective according to AF | The last used perspective of Project A | Actions of Project B | Project B perspective according to AF | The last used perspective of Project B | Required actions |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Create Project A | UAF | - | Create project B | MODAF | - | The project you are opening/creating requires the MODAF perspective. The perspective will be changed to MODAF automatically. (In the example below, Case #1) |
| Open Project A | UAF | - | Open project B | MODAF | - | The project you are opening/creating requires the MODAF perspective. The perspective will be changed to MODAF automatically. (In the example below, Case #1) |
| Migrate/Open/Create Project A | UAF | - | Migrate project B | MODAF | - | The project you are opening/creating requires the MODAF perspective. The perspective will be changed to MODAF automatically.(In the example below, Case #1) |
| Create Project A | UAF | - | Create project B | UAF | - | No actions |
| Open project A | UAF | - | Open project B | UAF | - | No actions |
| Migrate/Open/Create Project A | UAF | - | Migrate project B | UAF | - | No actions |
| Create Project A | UAF | MODAF | Create project B | MODAF | - | No actions |
| Open Project A | UAF | MODAF | Open project B | MODAF | - | No actions |
| Switch to opened Project A | UAF | MODAF | Switch to opened project B | UAF | - | The project you are opening/creating requires the UAF perspective. The perspective will be changed to UAF automatically.(In the example below, Case #1) |
| Switch to opened Project A | UAF | - | Switch to opened project B | NAF | - | The project you are opening/creating requires the NAF perspective. The perspective will be changed to NAF automatically.(In the example below, Case #1) |
| Switch to opened Project A | UAF | MODAF | Switch to opened project B | NAF | DoDAF 2 | The project you are opening requires the NAF perspective.To continue, choose one of the following:OK to switch the perspective to NAF (according to the framework).Cancel to switch the perspective to DoDAF 2(the latest used perspective).(In the example below, Case #2) |
| Switch to opened Project A | UAF | MODAF | Switch to opened project B | MODAF | DoDAF 2 | The project you are opening requires the MODAF perspective.To continue, choose one of the following:OK to switch the perspective to MODAF (according to the framework).Cancel to switch the perspective to DoDAF 2 (the latest used perspective).(In the example below, Case #2) |

##### Examples of messages

Here are examples of messages you receive when switching projects with different perspectives.

**Case #1.**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

**Case #2.**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249578332 space=VTO version=1 -->
## PAGE 00119: UAF 1.2

- page_id: `249578332`
- space_key: `VTO`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/VTO/pages/249578332/UAF+1.2
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-05-12T09:26:08.402+02:00`
- ancestors: Virtual Twin of the Organization Documentation > User Guide
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

For the documentation related to UAF 1.2, please see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation' space='UAF12PTWRT'].

The Unified Architecture Framework (UAF) supports the capability to:

- model architectures for a broad range of complex systems, which may include hardware, software, data, personnel, and facility elements;
- model consistent architectures for system-of-systems (SoS) down to lower levels of design and implementation;
- support the analysis, specification, design, and verification of complex systems; and
- improve the ability to exchange architecture information among related tools that are SysML-based and tools that are based on other standards.

According to modeling needs, there are the following UAF templates for different purposes:

- UAF Project for Virtual Twin of the Organization - you can create a project using this template to define a Virtual Twin of your Organization. APQC and ESCO model templates include the structure of processes and people skills relevant to organization modeling.
- UAF Project is designed to model architectures for a broad range of complex systems. The UAF provides an applicable model security controls, threat, risk, and risk mitigation. It allows for defining consistent architectures for System-of-Systems (SoS), including all creation phases from the design until implementation.
- EA with BPMN Project* - u sing this template, you will get a simplified Enterprise Architecture project with BPMN, which is based on the UAF framework and designed for enterprise and IT architecture modeling. Creating an EA with the BPMN project will switch the modeling tool to the EA-BPMN Architect graphical user interface and use the EA with the BPMN model template.
- **UAF Project Template with Process Guide** - using this template, you will get a new UAF project enhanced with a modeling guide. The modeling guide helps you to build architecture by providing summary information about the UAF structure and architecture creation process.
- **EA Project Template with Process Guide** - using this template, you will get a new UAF project enhanced with the Enterprise Architecture Guide for UAF (a non-normative component of the UAF specification). This modeling guide aids in building architecture by providing nine steps of workflow.
- **Blank UAF Project** - creates a blank UAF project.

Please make sure, that the UAF 1.2 Plugin is [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Installing plugins' space='ILTWRT'].

*Starting with the 2024x version, the **UAF Enterprise Architecture Project**was renamed to**EA with BPMN Project.**

****

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### UAF 1.2 Grid. The grid is a way of showing how the various view specifications (cells) correspond to viewpoints (prev. known as domains) (horizontal rows) and the aspects (prev. known as model kinds) (the columns) that describe the view specification.

The descriptions of all the viewpoints are provided in the following table:

| Viewpoint | Acronym | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Architecture Management | Am | Identifies the metadata required to develop a suitable architecture that is fit for its purpose. |
| Strategic | St | Capability management process. Describes the capability taxonomy, composition, dependencies, and evolution. |
| Operational | Op | Illustrates the Logical Architecture of the enterprise. Describes the requirements, operational behavior, structure, and exchanges required to support (exhibit) capabilities. Defines all operational elements in an implementation/solution-independent manner. |
| Services | Sv | The Service-Orientated View (SOV) is a description of services needed to directly support the operational domain as described in the Operational View. A service withinMODAF is understood in its broadest sense, as a unit of work through which a provider provides a useful result to a consumer.DoDAF: The Service Views within the Services Viewpoint describe the design for service-based solutions to support operational development processes (JCIDS) and Defense Acquisition System or capability development within the Joint Capability Areas. |
| Personnel | Ps | Defines and explores organizational resource types. Shows the taxonomy of types of organizational resources as well as connections, interaction, and growth over time. |
| Resources | Rs | Captures a solution architecture consisting of resources, e.g., organizational, software, artifacts, capability configurations, and natural resources that implement the operational requirements. Further design of a resource is typically detailed in SysML or UML. |
| Security | Sc | Security assets and security enclaves. Defines the hierarchy of security assets and asset owners, security constraints (policy, laws, and guidance), and details where they are located (security enclaves). |
| Projects | Pj | Describes projects and project milestones, how those projects deliver capabilities, the organizations contributing to the projects, and dependencies between projects. |
| Standards | Sd | MODAF: Technical Standards Views are extended from the core DoDAF views to include non-technical standards such as operational doctrine, industry process standards, etc.DoDAF: The Standards Views within the Standards Viewpoint are the set of rules governing the arrangement, interaction, and interdependence of solution parts or elements. |
| Actual Resources | Ar | The analysis, e.g., evaluation of different alternatives, what-if, trade-offs, V&V on the actual resource configurations. Illustrates the expected or achieved actual resource configurations. |

The descriptions of all the aspects are provided in the following table:

| Aspect | Acronym | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Motivation | Mv | Captures motivational elements e.g., challenges, opportunities, and concerns, that pertain to enterprise transformation efforts, and different types of requirements, e.g., operational, services, personnel, resources, or security controls. |
| Taxonomy | Tx | Presents all the elements as a standalone structure. Presents all the elements as a specialization hierarchy, provides a text definition for each one and references the source of the element. |
| Structure | Sr | Describes the breakdown of structural elements e.g. logical performers, systems, projects, etc. into their smaller parts. |
| Connectivity | Cn | Describes the connections, relationships, and interactions between the different elements. |
| Processes | Pr | Captures activity-based behavior and flows. It describes activities, their Inputs/Outputs, activity actions, and flows between them. |
| States | St | Captures state-based behavior of an element. It is a graphical representation of states of a structural element and how it responds to various events and actions. |
| Sequences | Sq | Expresses a time-ordered examination of the exchanges as a result of a particular scenario. Provides a time-ordered examination of the exchanges between participating elements as a result of a particular scenario. |
| Information | If | Address the information perspective on operational, service, and resource architectures. Allows analysis of an architecture’s information and data definition aspect, without consideration of implementation-specific issues. |
| Parameters | Pm | Shows the measurable properties of something in the physical world and elements and relationships that are involved in defining the environments applicable to capability, operational concept or set of systems. |
| Constraints | Ct | Details the measurements that set performance requirements constraining capabilities. Also defines the rules governing behavior and structure. |
| Roadmap | Rm | Addresses how elements in the architecture change over time. |
| Traceability | Tr | Describes the mapping between elements in the architecture. This can be between different viewpoints within domains as well as between domains. It can also be between structure and behaviors. |

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="946432f1-9db0-4346-8934-acc414e90add"><ac:rich-text-body><p>For the documentation related to UAF 1.2, please see the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12PTWRT" ri:content-title="UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation" /></ac:link>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="OMGNormalParagraph"><br /></p><p class="OMGNormalParagraph">The Unified Architecture Framework (UAF) supports the capability to:</p><ul><li>model architectures for a broad range of complex systems, which may include hardware, software, data, personnel, and facility elements;</li><li>model consistent architectures for system-of-systems (SoS) down to lower levels of design and implementation;</li><li>support the analysis, specification, design, and verification of complex systems; and</li><li>improve the ability to exchange architecture information among related tools that are SysML-based and tools that are based on other standards.</li></ul><p>According to modeling needs, there are the following UAF templates for different purposes:</p><ul><li><strong>UAF Project for Virtual Twin of the Organization</strong> - you can create a project using this template to define a Virtual Twin of your Organization. APQC and ESCO model templates include the structure of processes and people skills relevant to organization modeling.</li><li><strong>UAF Project</strong> is designed to model architectures for a broad range of complex systems. The UAF provides an applicable model security controls, threat, risk, and risk mitigation. It allows for defining consistent architectures for System-of-Systems (SoS), including all creation phases from the design until implementation.</li><li><strong>EA with BPMN Project* </strong>- u<span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">sing this template, you will get a simplified Enterprise Architecture project with BPMN, which is based on the UAF framework and designed for enterprise and IT architecture modeling. Creating an EA with the BPMN project will switch the modeling tool to the EA-BPMN Architect graphical user interface and use the EA with the BPMN model template.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);"><strong>UAF Project Template with Process Guide</strong> - using this template, you will get a new UAF project enhanced with a modeling guide. The modeling guide helps you to build architecture by providing summary information about the UAF structure and architecture creation process.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);"><strong>EA Project Template with Process Guide</strong> - using this template, you will get a new UAF project enhanced with the Enterprise Architecture Guide for UAF (a non-normative component of the UAF specification). This modeling guide aids in building architecture by providing nine steps of workflow.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);"><strong>Blank UAF Project</strong> - creates a blank UAF project.</span></li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="36302a13-0993-4e01-9260-10ef8aff2505"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Please make sure, that the UAF 1.2 Plugin is <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="ILTWRT" ri:content-title="Installing plugins" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[installed]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><hr /><p>*Starting with the 2024x version, the <strong style="text-align: left;">UAF Enterprise Architecture Project</strong><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);"><span> </span>was renamed to<span> </span></span><strong style="text-align: left;">EA with BPMN Project.</strong></p><p><strong style="text-align: left;">      </strong></p><p><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:width="1319"><ri:attachment ri:filename="UAF _1_2 grid Last.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="VTO" ri:content-title="UAF 1.2" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">UAF 1.2 Grid. The grid is a way of showing how the various view specifications (cells) correspond to viewpoints (prev. known as domains) (horizontal rows) and the aspects (prev. known as model kinds) (the columns) that describe the view specification. </h6><p>The descriptions of all the viewpoints are provided in the following table:</p><table class="relative-table wrapped" style="width: 93.8014%;"><colgroup class=""> <col class="" style="width: 7.89936%;" /> <col class="" style="width: 4.73961%;" /> <col class="" style="width: 87.361%;" /> </colgroup><tbody class=""><tr class=""><th><p align="center"><strong>Viewpoint</strong></p></th><th><p align="center"><strong>Acronym</strong></p></th><th><p align="center"><strong>Description</strong></p></th></tr><tr class=""><th><p>Architecture Management</p></th><td style="text-align: center;"><p>Am</p></td><td><p>Identifies the metadata required to develop a suitable architecture that is fit for its purpose.</p></td></tr><tr class=""><th><p>Strategic</p></th><td style="text-align: center;"><p>St</p></td><td><p>Capability management process. Describes the capability taxonomy, composition, dependencies, and evolution.</p></td></tr><tr class=""><th><p>Operational</p></th><td style="text-align: center;"><p>Op</p></td><td><p>Illustrates the Logical Architecture of the enterprise. Describes the requirements, operational behavior, structure, and exchanges required to support (exhibit) capabilities. Defines all operational elements in an implementation/solution-independent manner.</p></td></tr><tr class=""><th><p>Services</p></th><td style="text-align: center;"><p>Sv</p></td><td><p>The Service-Orientated View (SOV) is a description of services needed to directly support the operational domain as described in the Operational View. A service within</p><p>MODAF is understood in its broadest sense, as a unit of work through which a provider provides a useful result to a consumer.</p><p>DoDAF: The Service Views within the Services Viewpoint describe the design for service-based solutions to support operational development processes (JCIDS) and Defense Acquisition System or capability development within the Joint Capability Areas.</p></td></tr><tr class=""><th><p>Personnel</p></th><td style="text-align: center;"><p>Ps</p></td><td><p>Defines and explores organizational resource types. Shows the taxonomy of types of organizational resources as well as connections, interaction, and growth over time.</p></td></tr><tr class=""><th><p>Resources</p></th><td style="text-align: center;"><p>Rs</p></td><td><p>Captures a solution architecture consisting of resources, e.g., organizational, software, artifacts, capability configurations, and natural resources that implement the operational requirements. Further design of a resource is typically detailed in SysML or UML.</p></td></tr><tr class=""><th><p>Security</p></th><td style="text-align: center;"><p>Sc</p></td><td><p>Security assets and security enclaves. Defines the hierarchy of security assets and asset owners, security constraints (policy, laws, and guidance), and details where they are located (security enclaves).</p></td></tr><tr class=""><th><p>Projects</p></th><td style="text-align: center;"><p>Pj</p></td><td><p>Describes projects and project milestones, how those projects deliver capabilities, the organizations contributing to the projects, and dependencies between projects.</p></td></tr><tr class=""><th><p>Standards</p></th><td style="text-align: center;"><p>Sd</p></td><td><p>MODAF: Technical Standards Views are extended from the core DoDAF views to include non-technical standards such as operational doctrine, industry process standards, etc.</p><p>DoDAF: The Standards Views within the Standards Viewpoint are the set of rules governing the arrangement, interaction, and interdependence of solution parts or elements.</p></td></tr><tr class=""><th><p>Actual Resources</p></th><td style="text-align: center;"><p>Ar</p></td><td><p>The analysis, e.g., evaluation of different alternatives, what-if, trade-offs, V&amp;V on the actual resource configurations. Illustrates the expected or achieved actual resource configurations.</p></td></tr></tbody></table><p><br /></p><p>The descriptions of all the aspects are provided in the following table:</p><table class="relative-table wrapped" style="width: 94.0208%;"><colgroup class=""><col class="" style="width: 7.82253%;" /><col class="" style="width: 5.02043%;" /><col class="" style="width: 87.157%;" /></colgroup><tbody class=""><tr class=""><th style="text-align: center;">Aspect</th><th style="text-align: center;">Acronym</th><th style="text-align: center;">Description</th></tr><tr class=""><th colspan="1">Motivation</th><td colspan="1" style="text-align: center;">Mv</td><td colspan="1">Captures motivational elements e.g., challenges, opportunities, and concerns, that pertain to enterprise transformation efforts, and different types of requirements, e.g., operational, services, personnel, resources, or security controls.</td></tr><tr class=""><th>Taxonomy</th><td style="text-align: center;">Tx</td><td><p>Presents all the elements as a standalone structure. Presents all the elements as a specialization hierarchy, provides a text definition for each one and references the<strong> </strong>source of the element.</p></td></tr><tr class=""><th>Structure</th><td style="text-align: center;">Sr</td><td><p>Describes the breakdown of structural elements e.g. logical performers, systems, projects, etc. into their smaller parts.</p></td></tr><tr class=""><th>Connectivity</th><td style="text-align: center;">Cn</td><td><p>Describes the connections, relationships, and interactions between the different elements.</p></td></tr><tr class=""><th colspan="1">Processes</th><td colspan="1" style="text-align: center;">Pr</td><td colspan="1"><p>Captures activity-based behavior and flows. It describes activities, their Inputs/Outputs, activity actions, and flows between them.</p></td></tr><tr class=""><th colspan="1">States</th><td colspan="1" style="text-align: center;">St</td><td colspan="1"><p>Captures state-based behavior of an element. It is a graphical representation of states of a structural element and how it responds to various events and actions.</p></td></tr><tr class=""><th colspan="1">Sequences</th><td colspan="1" style="text-align: center;">Sq</td><td colspan="1"><p>Expresses a time-ordered examination of the exchanges as a result of a particular scenario. Provides a time-ordered examination of the exchanges between participating elements as a result of a particular scenario.</p></td></tr><tr class=""><th colspan="1">Information</th><td colspan="1" style="text-align: center;">If</td><td colspan="1"><p>Address the information perspective on operational, service, and resource architectures. Allows analysis of an architecture’s information and data definition aspect, without consideration of implementation-specific issues.</p></td></tr><tr class=""><th colspan="1">Parameters</th><td colspan="1" style="text-align: center;">Pm</td><td colspan="1">Shows the measurable properties of something in the physical world and elements and relationships that are involved in defining the environments applicable to capability, operational concept or set of systems.</td></tr><tr class=""><th colspan="1">Constraints</th><td colspan="1" style="text-align: center;">Ct</td><td colspan="1"><p>Details the measurements that set performance requirements constraining capabilities. Also defines the rules governing behavior and structure.</p></td></tr><tr class=""><th colspan="1">Roadmap</th><td colspan="1" style="text-align: center;">Rm</td><td colspan="1"><p>Addresses how elements in the architecture change over time.</p></td></tr><tr class=""><th colspan="1">Traceability</th><td colspan="1" style="text-align: center;">Tr</td><td colspan="1"><p>Describes the mapping between elements in the architecture. This can be between different viewpoints within domains as well as between domains. It can also be between structure and behaviors.</p></td></tr></tbody></table>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249578338 space=VTO version=1 -->
## PAGE 00122: Using APQC classification

- page_id: `249578338`
- space_key: `VTO`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/VTO/pages/249578338/Using+APQC+classification
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-05-13T07:25:10.512+02:00`
- ancestors: Virtual Twin of the Organization Documentation > User Guide > Procedures
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**APQC (American Productivity & Quality Center)** is a leading authority on benchmarking, best practices, process and performance improvement, and knowledge management (KM).

**APQC’s Process Classification Framework® (PCF)** is a taxonomy of business processes that allows organizations to objectively track and compare their performance internally and externally with organizations from any industry.

The VTO plug-in includes data from **PCF version 7.4.0** that is ready to use for the Enterprise Architect. This data is organized using two notations:

- **APQC-PCF classification:** top level structure organized in packages****

**L1. Category:**the highest level of the process framework. Thirteen Categories have been defined, such as “Develop and Manage Products and Services” or “Develop and Manage Human Capital."

**L2. Process Group:**indicates a group of processes that are part of executing a Category.

- **Ready-to-use APQC-PCF templates:** a low-level structure modeled with Standard Operational Activities.

**L3. Process:** a single process; it is the name for a series of steps required to achieve a particular result

**L4. Activity:** a key step performed to execute a process.

**L5. Task**: an element of work that goes into executing an activity. Tasks are the most fine-grained elements of the PCF, and they often vary among industries and organizations.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><strong>APQC (American Productivity &amp; Quality Center)</strong> is a leading authority on benchmarking, best practices, process and performance improvement, and knowledge management (KM).</p><p><strong>APQC’s Process Classification Framework® (PCF)</strong> is a taxonomy of business processes that allows organizations to objectively track and compare their performance internally and externally with organizations from any industry. </p><p>The VTO plug-in includes data from <strong>PCF version 7.4.0</strong> that is ready to use for the Enterprise Architect. This data is organized using two notations:</p><ul><li><p><strong>APQC-PCF classification:</strong> top level structure organized in packages<strong><br /></strong></p></li></ul><p style="margin-left: 40.0px;"><strong>L1. Category:<span> </span></strong>the highest level of the process framework. Thirteen Categories have been defined, such as “Develop and Manage Products and Services” or “Develop and Manage Human Capital.&quot;</p><p style="margin-left: 40.0px;"><strong>L2. Process Group:<span> </span></strong>indicates a group of processes that are part of executing a Category.</p><ul><li><p><strong>Ready-to-use APQC-PCF templates:</strong> a low-level structure modeled with Standard Operational Activities.</p></li></ul><p style="margin-left: 40.0px;"><strong>L3. Process<span>:</span></strong> a single process; it is the name for a series of steps required to achieve a particular result</p><p style="margin-left: 40.0px;"><strong>L4. Activity<span>:</span></strong> a key step performed to execute a process.</p><p style="margin-left: 40.0px;"><strong>L5. Task</strong>: an element of work that goes into executing an activity. Tasks are the most fine-grained elements of the PCF, and they often vary among industries and organizations.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="APQC data.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="VTO" ri:content-title="Using APQC classification" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249578340 space=VTO version=1 -->
## PAGE 00123: Using ESCO classification

- page_id: `249578340`
- space_key: `VTO`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/VTO/pages/249578340/Using+ESCO+classification
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-05-12T18:50:26.780+02:00`
- ancestors: Virtual Twin of the Organization Documentation > User Guide > Procedures
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**ESCO (European Skills, Competences, Qualifications and Occupations)** is the European multilingual classification of Skills, Competencies, and Occupations.

ESCO works as a **dictionary,** describing, identifying, and classifying professional occupations and skills relevant to the labor market, along with education and training.

Using ESCO provides multiple values:

- Improved Job Matching & Skills Development: Standardized terminology for skills and business roles enhancing job matching, reducing skills gaps, and supporting personalized learning paths.
- Cross-Border and Cross-Value Chain Consistency & Mobility: Multilingual standardization facilitates the recognition of skills and qualifications across countries, boosting workforce mobility, and standardizing HR processes.
- Data-Driven Insights & Policy Alignment: Roles and skills standardization enables access to labor market data for organizational process benchmarking.

The VTO plug-in includes data from **ESCO version 1.2.0** that is ready to use for the Enterprise Architect. The data is organized into two categories: Occupations (aka roles), and Competencies (aka skills). Relations have been imported between roles and skills, but only essential skills have been considered.

UAF types from the Personnel viewpoint are used for representing ESCO data: **UAF Post**for modeling ESCO Occupations, and **UAF Competence** for modeling ESCO Competencies. 
Relations use the **UAF Requires Competence** relation.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><strong>ESCO (European Skills, Competences, Qualifications and Occupations)</strong> is the European multilingual classification of Skills, Competencies, and Occupations.  </p><p>ESCO works as a <strong>dictionary,</strong> describing, identifying, and classifying professional occupations and skills relevant to the labor market, along with education and training. </p><p>Using ESCO provides multiple values:</p><ul><li><strong>Improved Job Matching &amp; Skills Development:</strong><span> </span>Standardized terminology for skills and business roles enhancing job matching, reducing skills gaps, and supporting personalized learning paths.</li><li><strong>Cross-Border and Cross-Value Chain Consistency &amp; Mobility:</strong><span> </span>Multilingual standardization facilitates the recognition of skills and qualifications across countries, boosting workforce mobility, and standardizing HR processes.</li><li><strong>Data-Driven Insights &amp; Policy Alignment:</strong><span> </span>Roles and skills standardization enables access to labor market data for organizational process benchmarking.</li></ul><p>The VTO plug-in includes data from <strong>ESCO version 1.2.0</strong> that is ready to use for the Enterprise Architect. The data is organized into two categories: Occupations (aka roles), and Competencies (aka skills). Relations have been imported between roles and skills, but only essential skills have been considered.</p><p>UAF types from the Personnel viewpoint are used for representing ESCO data: <strong>UAF Post </strong>for modeling ESCO Occupations, and <strong>UAF Competence</strong> for modeling ESCO Competencies.<br />Relations use the <strong>UAF Requires Competence</strong> relation.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="ESCO data.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="VTO" ri:content-title="Using ESCO classification" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249578292 space=VTO version=1 -->
## PAGE 00126: Virtual Twin of the Organization Documentation

- page_id: `249578292`
- space_key: `VTO`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/VTO/pages/249578292/Virtual+Twin+of+the+Organization+Documentation
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-05-12T18:26:30.873+02:00`
- ancestors: 
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Govern the strategic business transformation from modeling and simulation leveraging the virtual twin of enterprise transformation.**

Using the**Virtual Twin of the Organization (VTO) plug-in**, the**Enterprise Architects**will benefit from the formal**modelling**of the enterprise and necessary resources, leveraging:

- UAF (Unified Architecture Framework) to provide a holistic view of complex organizations, ensuring alignment across multiple domains while offering traceability, impact analysis, and flexibility.
- APQC for consistent process modeling across departments, functions and regions, to identify inefficiencies, enhance performance, and align processes with business objectives and strategies
- ESCO to align workforce skills and job roles with organization needs

From the enterprise model created in CATIA Magic, using the**Collaborative Designer for CATIA Magic role**, data can be pushed to the**3DEXPERIENCE Platform**.

**VTO Data Perspectives**will leverage Virtual Twin data and allow **n****avigating the enterprise operational model** for a better understanding of how people in business fit into this framework. It will also enhance collaboration with colleagues, by revealing the contribution of each role to enterprise processes and deliverables globally or in the context of a project.

For more information about working with VTO, see:

1

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><strong>Govern the strategic business transformation from modeling and simulation leveraging the virtual twin of enterprise transformation.</strong></p><p>Using the<span> </span><strong>Virtual Twin of the Organization (VTO) plug-in</strong>, the<span> </span><strong>Enterprise Architects</strong><span> </span>will benefit from the formal<span> </span><strong>modelling<span> </span></strong>of the enterprise and necessary resources, leveraging:</p><ul><li><strong>UAF<span> </span></strong>(Unified Architecture Framework) to provide a holistic view of complex organizations, ensuring alignment across multiple domains while offering traceability, impact analysis, and flexibility.</li><li><strong>APQC<span> </span></strong>for consistent process modeling across departments, functions and regions, to identify inefficiencies, enhance performance, and align processes with business objectives and strategies </li><li><strong>ESCO<span> </span></strong>to align workforce skills and job roles with organization needs</li></ul><p>From the enterprise model created in CATIA Magic, using the<strong><span> </span>Collaborative Designer for CATIA Magic role</strong>, data can be pushed to the<span> </span><strong>3DEXPERIENCE Platform</strong>.</p><p><strong>VTO Data Perspectives</strong><span> </span>will leverage Virtual Twin data and allow <strong>n</strong><span style="color: rgb(61,61,61);"><strong>avigating the enterprise operational model</strong> for a better understanding of how people in business fit into this framework. It will also enhance collaboration with colleagues, by revealing the contribution of each role to enterprise processes and deliverables globally or in the context of a project.</span></p><p><br /></p><p>For more information about working with VTO, see:</p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="3ada6501-6955-4f62-a409-1ab429a46a17"><ac:parameter ac:name="depth">1</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p><p><br /></p><p><br /></p><p><br /></p><p>       </p>
````
